reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM; SAP S/4HANA PP; IATF 16949

description: 17-character Vehicle Identification Number (VIN) assigned to the vehicle at this sequence position, conforming to ISO 3779 and NHTSA standards. May be pre-assigned at sequence creation or stamped during body shop operations. Critical for build traceability and regulatory compliance.
Unique surrogate identifier for each downtime event record in the manufacturing execution system. Primary key for the downtime_event data product.
reference: IATF 16949 Section 9.1 – Monitoring, Measurement, Analysis and Evaluation

description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: OEE-related downtime costs (lost production, emergency maintenance labor) are allocated to cost centers for manufacturing overhead variance analysis and plant P&L reporting. Automotive plant controlle

Foreign key linking to quality.defect_record. Business justification: Downtime events with is_quality_hold_triggered=true reference a defect record. Linking enables root cause analysis connecting equipment failure to quality impact, supporting OEE and quality KPI report

Foreign key linking to supply.inbound_part. Business justification: Supplier-caused downtime events must be traceable to the specific inbound part for supplier corrective action requests, scorecard impact calculation, and warranty cost recovery. This is a standard aut
Reference to the manufacturing plant where the downtime event occurred. Corresponds to the SAP Plant (Werk) organizational unit.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP – Plant (Werk); IATF 16949 Section 4.3

description: Reference to the production line (e.g., stamping line, body shop line, paint line, final assembly line) affected by the downtime event. Enables line-level OEE and throughput impact analysis.
reference: Apriso/Dassault MES – Production Line; SAP S/4HANA PP – Production Line

description: Reference to the SAP PP Production Order (Fertigungsauftrag) that was active or impacted at the time of the downtime event. Used to quantify production order impact and schedule deviation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP – Production Order; IATF 16949 Section 8.5

description: Foreign key linking to manufacturing.shift. Business justification: Downtime events occur during a specific production shift. The shift_code STRING column is a denormalized reference to the shift master. Replacing it with shift_id FK enables proper shift-level downtim

Reference to the specific work center or production cell where the downtime event was recorded. Maps to SAP PP Work Center (Arbeitsplatz) or MES work station definition. Critical for OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness) analysis at the work center level.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP – Work Center; Apriso MES – Work Center; IATF 16949 Section 9.1

description: The manufacturing process area impacted by the downtime event. Reflects the major shop segments in automotive assembly: stamping (press shop), body shop (BIW - Body in White), paint shop, final assembly, powertrain assembly, chassis, trim, or general assembly. Used for plant-level downtime distribution analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: stamping|body_shop|paint|final_assembly|powertrain|chassis|trim|general_assembly — promote to reference product]
reference: Apriso/Dassault MES – Process Area; IATF 16949 Section 8.5; SAP PP – Production Process

description: Standardized code from the SAP PM activity type catalog describing the corrective action taken to resolve the downtime event (e.g., part replacement, lubrication, calibration, software reset, tooling adjustment). Supports FMEA corrective action tracking and spare parts consumption analysis.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PM – Activity Type Code; IATF 16949 Section 10.2; AIAG 8D

description: The timestamp when the downtime event record was first created in the source system (MES, SAP PM, or SCADA). Represents the audit creation time, distinct from start_timestamp which is the actual business event time. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: IATF 16949 Section 7.5 – Documented Information; SAP S/4HANA – Record Creation Timestamp

description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the maintenance_cost_local field (e.g., USD, EUR, MXN, CNY). Required for multi-plant, multi-currency cost consolidation in global automotive manufacturing operations.
reference: ISO 4217 – Currency Codes; SAP S/4HANA FI – Currency Configuration

description: Primary classification of the downtime event type. 'mechanical_failure' covers equipment breakdowns; 'tooling_change' covers die/tool swap activities; 'material_shortage' covers JIT/JIS supply disruptions; 'quality_hold' covers production stops due to quality issues; 'scheduled_maintenance' covers planned PM activities; 'changeover' covers model or variant changeover activities. Drives OEE availability loss categorization.
reference: IATF 16949 Section 9.1 – OEE; SAP PM – Notification Type; ISO 22400-2 – KPI for Manufacturing

description: Indicates whether the downtime event was planned (scheduled maintenance, changeover, tooling change per production schedule) or unplanned (unexpected breakdown, quality hold, material shortage). Critical for separating planned vs. unplanned OEE availability losses.
reference: ISO 22400-2 – Planned vs. Unplanned Downtime; IATF 16949 Section 9.1

description: Total elapsed duration of the downtime event in minutes, calculated as the difference between end_timestamp and start_timestamp. Stored as a business field for direct use in OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness) availability calculations and SCADA-integrated downtime reporting. Null if the event has not yet ended.
reference: ISO 22400-2 – Downtime Duration; IATF 16949 Section 9.1; SAP PM – Breakdown Duration

description: The exact date and time when the downtime event ended and production resumed, as recorded by the MES or SCADA system. Null if the event is still active. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: Apriso/Dassault MES – Downtime End; SCADA Event Log; ISO 22400-2

description: Indicates the management escalation level reached during the downtime event based on duration thresholds or safety/quality triggers. 'none' = resolved at operator level; 'supervisor' = shift supervisor notified; 'manager' = production manager engaged; 'plant_director' = plant director escalation triggered. Supports escalation SLA compliance reporting.
reference: IATF 16949 Section 5.3 – Organizational Roles; SAP PM – Notification Priority; Internal Escalation Matrix

description: The calendar date on which the downtime event started. Derived from start_timestamp for day-level partitioning, reporting, and trend analysis. Stored as a separate DATE field to support efficient date-range queries and daily OEE dashboard aggregations. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.
reference: ISO 22400-2 – Downtime Reporting; SAP S/4HANA PP – Production Date

description: Free-text narrative description of the downtime event as entered by the operator, technician, or supervisor in the MES or SAP PM notification. Captures contextual details not covered by structured codes, such as observed symptoms, environmental conditions, or operator observations at the time of failure.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PM – Notification Long Text; Apriso MES – Event Notes; IATF 16949 Section 10.2

description: Externally-known, human-readable business identifier for the downtime event, typically generated by the MES (Manufacturing Execution System) or SAP PM (Plant Maintenance) module. Format: DT-{YYYY}-{NNNNNN}. Used for cross-system referencing and shop floor communication.
reference: Apriso/Dassault MES – Downtime Event Reference; SAP PM Notification Number

description: Current lifecycle status of the downtime event record. 'open' indicates the event is active and unresolved; 'in_progress' indicates a repair or corrective action is underway; 'resolved' indicates the root cause has been addressed; 'closed' indicates the event is fully documented and closed; 'cancelled' indicates the event was logged in error.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PM – Notification Status; Apriso MES – Downtime Status; IATF 16949 Section 10.2

description: Standardized code identifying the specific failure mode that caused the downtime event, aligned with the FMEA (Failure Mode and Effects Analysis) failure mode library. Examples: electrical fault, hydraulic leak, sensor failure, conveyor jam. Enables FMEA-driven root cause trending and PPAP (Production Part Approval Process) corrective action tracking.
reference: IATF 16949 Section 10.2 – FMEA; SAP PM – Damage Code; AIAG FMEA Reference Manual

description: SAP PM object part / location code identifying the specific component or sub-assembly on the equipment where the fault occurred (e.g., spindle, conveyor belt, welding gun tip, paint nozzle). Enables component-level failure frequency analysis and spare parts demand forecasting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PM – Object Part Code; ISO 14224 – Equipment Taxonomy; IATF 16949 Section 9.1

description: Indicates whether the downtime event triggered a quality hold on WIP (Work in Progress) or finished vehicle inventory. True = quality hold was activated; False = no quality hold. Links to SAP QM quality notification and SPC (Statistical Process Control) out-of-control action plans.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM – Quality Notification; IATF 16949 Section 8.6; SPC AIAG Reference Manual

description: Indicates whether this downtime event is a recurrence of a previously recorded failure on the same equipment or work center within a defined lookback period (typically 30 days). True = repeat failure; False = first occurrence. Used to identify chronic equipment issues requiring escalated corrective action under IATF 16949 Section 10.2.
reference: IATF 16949 Section 10.2 – Nonconformity and Corrective Action; SAP PM – Recurring Breakdown

description: Indicates whether the downtime event involved a safety incident, near-miss, or safety-critical equipment failure (e.g., AGV collision, robot safety stop, lockout/tagout activation). True = safety-related; False = non-safety. Triggers mandatory safety reporting and OSHA/DOT compliance escalation workflows.
reference: OSHA 29 CFR 1910 – Machine Safety; DOT FMVSS; IATF 16949 Section 8.4.2

description: The timestamp when the downtime event record was most recently modified in the source system, such as when resolution notes were added, status was updated, or cost data was posted. Used for incremental data pipeline processing and audit trail in the Silver layer. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: IATF 16949 Section 7.5 – Documented Information; Databricks Lakehouse – Delta Lake Change Data Capture

description: Total direct maintenance cost incurred to resolve the downtime event, including labor, spare parts, and external service costs, expressed in the plant's local currency. Sourced from SAP PM actual cost settlement. Used for cost-of-downtime analysis and CapEx vs. OpEx maintenance budget reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PM – Actual Cost; SAP CO – Cost Center Accounting; IATF 16949 Section 9.1

description: The vehicle model year (MY) of the production program running on the affected line at the time of the downtime event. Used to correlate downtime patterns with specific model year launches, SOP (Start of Production) ramp-up periods, and EOP (End of Production) transitions.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP – Production Program; NHTSA MY Definition; Siemens Teamcenter PLM – Model Year

description: The SAP Plant Maintenance (PM) notification number (Meldungsnummer) created in SAP S/4HANA to formally record the maintenance request or breakdown notification associated with this downtime event. Enables traceability between the MES downtime record and the SAP PM work order lifecycle.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PM – PM Notification (IW21/IW22); IATF 16949 Section 7.1.3

description: The percentage of scheduled production time lost due to this downtime event, calculated as (duration_minutes / scheduled_production_time_minutes) × 100. Represents the availability loss component of the OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness) metric for the affected work center during the event period. Stored as a business field for direct use in OEE dashboards.
reference: ISO 22400-2 – OEE Availability; IATF 16949 Section 9.1; SEMI E10 Standard

description: The powertrain type of the vehicle being produced at the time of the downtime event. ICE = Internal Combustion Engine, HEV = Hybrid Electric Vehicle, PHEV = Plug-in Hybrid Electric Vehicle, EV = Electric Vehicle, FCEV = Fuel Cell Electric Vehicle. Used to analyze downtime patterns by powertrain technology, particularly relevant for EV/PHEV line ramp-up monitoring.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP – Material Classification; CAFE/EPA Powertrain Classification; UNECE Regulation

description: Number of vehicle units or production units lost due to the downtime event, based on the planned production rate for the affected work center. Used to quantify the production order impact and schedule recovery requirements.
reference: ISO 22400-2 – Production Loss; SAP S/4HANA PP – Production Order Confirmation; IATF 16949 Section 9.1

description: Free-text field capturing the maintenance technician's description of the actions taken to resolve the downtime event and restore production. Complements corrective_action_code with detailed narrative. Stored in the Silver layer for use in AI/ML-based predictive maintenance model training.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PM – Work Order Completion Text; IATF 16949 Section 10.2; AIAG 8D Step 5

description: Standardized code identifying the confirmed root cause of the downtime event after investigation, aligned with the SAP PM cause code catalog. Distinct from failure_mode_code which captures the symptom; root_cause_code captures the underlying reason (e.g., inadequate lubrication, worn tooling, incorrect setup, supplier part defect).
reference: SAP S/4HANA PM – Cause Code; IATF 16949 Section 10.2; AIAG 8D Problem Solving

description: The alarm or fault code generated by the SCADA (Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition) or PLC (Programmable Logic Controller) system at the time the downtime event was triggered. Provides the raw machine-level signal that initiated the downtime record. Used for SCADA-integrated downtime reporting and automated event correlation.
reference: IEC 62682 – Management of Alarm Systems; SCADA/PLC Event Log; Apriso MES – Machine Signal Integration

description: Identifies the operational system of record from which the downtime event was originated or captured. MES = Apriso/Dassault Manufacturing Execution System (automated); SAP_PM = SAP Plant Maintenance notification (manual or integrated); SCADA = SCADA system automated alarm; PLC = PLC-generated fault signal; MANUAL = manually entered by operator. Used for data lineage and source system reconciliation in the Silver layer.
reference: Apriso/Dassault MES; SAP S/4HANA PM; SCADA/PLC Integration; Databricks Lakehouse Data Lineage

description: Indicates whether spare parts or materials were consumed from inventory to resolve the downtime event. True = spare parts were issued via SAP MM goods issue; False = no parts consumed (e.g., adjustment or reset only). Drives spare parts demand planning and MRP (Material Requirements Planning) replenishment.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM – Goods Issue; SAP PM – Material Consumption; MRP APICS Standard

description: The exact date and time when the downtime event began, as recorded by the MES or SCADA system. This is the principal real-world event time used for duration calculation and shift-level reporting. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: Apriso/Dassault MES – Downtime Start; SCADA Event Log; ISO 22400-2

description: Elapsed time in minutes from when the maintenance team began active repair work to when the equipment was restored to operational status. Distinct from duration_minutes (total downtime) and time_to_respond_minutes (response lag). Core component of MTTR (Mean Time to Repair) KPI calculation.
reference: ISO 22400-2 – MTTR; SAP PM – Repair Duration; IATF 16949 Section 9.1

description: Elapsed time in minutes between the downtime event start_timestamp and the time the responsible maintenance or production team acknowledged and began responding to the event. Key metric for maintenance response SLA (Service Level Agreement) compliance and MTTR (Mean Time to Repair) component analysis.
reference: IATF 16949 Section 9.1; SAP PM – Response Time; ISO 22400-2 – MTTR

description: The internal vehicle model or platform code (e.g., F-150, Silverado, Model 3 equivalent) being produced on the affected line at the time of the downtime event. Enables model-specific downtime analysis and supports PLM (Product Lifecycle Management) integration.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM – Vehicle Program Code; SAP S/4HANA PP – Material Master

description: The SAP PM maintenance work order number (Auftragsnummer) issued to address the downtime event. Distinct from the production order; this is the corrective or preventive maintenance order raised by the maintenance team in SAP PM.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PM – Maintenance Order (IW31/IW32); IATF 16949 Section 8.5

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the production confirmation record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Primary key for this entity. Entity Role: TRANSACTION_HEADER — represents a discrete business event (completion of a production order or partial operation) with a clear lifecycle.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module — CO11N/CO15 Confirmation Object

description: Foreign key linking to manufacturing.build_stage. Business justification: A production_confirmation records completion of a specific operation at a build stage. The assembly_stage STRING is a denormalized reference to the build_stage master. Adding build_stage_id FK enables

Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Activity confirmations post actual labor and machine costs to cost centers in the SAP CO module. production_confirmation.cost_center_code is a denormalized plain attribute; replacing it with a proper

Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Production confirmations trigger goods receipt and activity cost postings in SAP; they must be tied to fiscal periods for period-end cutoff, production cost settlement, and monthly closing. Automotive

Foreign key linking to quality.inspection_lot. Business justification: SAP QM standard: production confirmations create inspection lots for in-process quality. Existing 'quality_inspection_lot' is a denormalized lot number; proper FK to inspection_lot.inspection_lot_id e
Reference to the manufacturing plant where this production confirmation was posted. Enables plant-level production output reporting, capacity utilization analysis, and multi-plant benchmarking.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP — Plant (WERKS); ISO 14001 Facility Identification

description: Foreign key linking to manufacturing.production_order. Business justification: A production_confirmation confirms the completion of a production_order. The production_order_number STRING column is a denormalized reference; replacing it with a proper FK production_order_id normal

Reference to the original production confirmation record that this entry reverses. Populated only when reversal_indicator is True. Enables audit trail reconstruction and net quantity calculation when reversals are present. Self-referencing within the production_confirmation product.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP — CO13 Reversal Reference; IATF 16949 Clause 8.5.2 Traceability

description: Reference to the production shift during which this confirmation was recorded. Enables shift-level production output analysis, yield comparison across shifts, and workforce productivity reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP — Shift (SCHICHT); Apriso MES Shift Management

description: Foreign key linking to inventory.storage_location. Business justification: Production confirmations post goods receipts to specific storage locations (SAP GR posting). storage_location_code is a denormalized reference. This FK is required for accurate inventory posting, WIP-

Reference to the work center (SAP ARBPL) where the confirmed operation was executed. Work centers represent physical production resources such as stamping presses, welding stations, paint booths, or assembly lines. Used for capacity reporting and OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness) analysis.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP — Work Center (ARBPL); IATF 16949 Clause 7.1.3 Infrastructure

description: SAP CO activity type (LSTAR) used to record the confirmed actual activities (setup, machine, labor) against the production order. Drives cost settlement rates and actual cost calculation in SAP CO-PC (Product Costing). Required for standard vs. actual cost variance analysis.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-PC — Activity Type (LSTAR); IATF 16949 Clause 9.1.3

description: The unit of measure in which yield, scrap, and rework quantities are expressed. Typically 'EA' (Each) for vehicles and assemblies, 'PC' (Piece) for components, 'KG' for weight-based materials. Maps to SAP MM/PP base unit of measure (MEINS). Required for correct inventory posting and quantity reconciliation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|PC|KG|L|M|SET|VH — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Base Unit of Measure (MEINS); ISO 80000 Quantities and Units

description: SAP batch number (CHARG) assigned to the confirmed production output. Used for batch-managed materials to enable full forward and backward traceability of components and finished vehicles. Critical for recall management, warranty analysis, and regulatory compliance with NHTSA traceability requirements.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM/PP — Batch Number (CHARG); NHTSA 49 CFR Part 573 Recall Traceability; IATF 16949 Clause 8.5.2

description: Method by which this production confirmation was entered into SAP PP. 'manual' = entered by operator via CO11N; 'automatic' = system-generated via PP-PI or SCADA integration; 'mes_interface' = posted via Apriso/Dassault MES interface; 'backflush' = automatic backflush confirmation triggered by goods issue. Used for data quality auditing and process automation tracking.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP — Confirmation Entry Mode; Apriso MES Integration; SCADA Interface

description: Externally-known alphanumeric confirmation number assigned by SAP PP (CO11N) at the time of posting. This is the business-facing identifier used in shop floor reporting, MES integration, and order settlement reconciliation. Maps to SAP field RUECK (Confirmation Number).
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP — CO11N Confirmation Number (RUECK); IATF 16949 Clause 8.5.2 Traceability

description: Current lifecycle status of the production confirmation record. 'created' = confirmation entered but not yet posted; 'posted' = successfully posted to SAP PP and inventory updated; 'reversed' = confirmation reversed via CO13; 'cancelled' = voided before posting; 'partial' = partial quantity confirmed, order still open; 'final' = final confirmation closing the production order.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP — Confirmation Status (CO11N/CO13); IATF 16949 Clause 8.5.1

description: The principal real-world event timestamp recording when the production operation or order was confirmed as complete on the shop floor. This is the business event time (not the system posting time) and represents the actual completion moment. Maps to SAP PP fields BUDAT (posting date) + ISDD/ISDZ (actual execution date/time). Used for takt time analysis, shift output reporting, and production scheduling.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP — CO11N Actual Execution Timestamp (ISDD/ISDZ); IATF 16949 Clause 8.5.2

description: Classification of the confirmation indicating whether it is a partial confirmation (interim progress), final confirmation (order completion), automatic system-generated confirmation, milestone-based confirmation, or a reversal entry. Determines downstream SAP PP processing logic including order closure and goods receipt.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP — Confirmation Category (STOKZ); Apriso MES Confirmation Type

description: The timestamp when this production confirmation record was first created in the source system (SAP PP or MES). Represents the system audit creation time, distinct from the confirmation_timestamp (business event time). Used for data lineage, audit trail, and SLA monitoring of confirmation posting latency.
reference: SAP S/4HANA — Record Creation Timestamp; IATF 16949 Clause 7.5 Documented Information

description: Total unplanned downtime duration recorded during this production confirmation period, in minutes. Captured from SCADA/PLC systems or MES downtime tracking. Used for OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness) availability calculation, maintenance planning, and production loss analysis.
reference: Apriso/Dassault MES — Downtime Tracking; SCADA Integration; IATF 16949 Clause 7.1.3

description: Coded reason for any downtime recorded during this confirmation period. Maps to the plant's downtime reason catalog (e.g., 'MECH' = Mechanical Failure, 'MATL' = Material Shortage, 'QUAL' = Quality Hold, 'CHNG' = Changeover). Used for root cause analysis, maintenance prioritization, and production loss reporting.
reference: Apriso/Dassault MES — Downtime Reason Catalog; IATF 16949 Clause 7.1.3; SAP PM Integration

description: Indicates whether this confirmation is the final confirmation for the production order (SAP PP IEDD — Final Confirmation Indicator). When True, SAP PP automatically closes the production order, triggers goods receipt for the finished product, and initiates order settlement to CO (Controlling). Critical for order lifecycle management and inventory accuracy.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP — Final Confirmation Indicator (IEDD); IATF 16949 Clause 8.5.1

description: Indicates whether the automatic goods receipt (GR) to inventory has been successfully posted as a result of this production confirmation. True = inventory has been updated with the confirmed yield quantity. Used for inventory reconciliation and to identify confirmations where GR posting failed or is pending.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP/MM — Goods Receipt (MIGO/MB31); IATF 16949 Clause 8.5.1

description: Actual direct labor time consumed for this production operation, recorded in minutes. Maps to SAP PP activity type for labor (ISRLA — Actual Labor Time). Used for labor cost settlement to the production order, workforce productivity analysis, and standard vs. actual cost variance reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP/CO — Actual Labor Time (ISRLA); IATF 16949 Clause 7.1.2 People

description: Planned direct labor time for this operation as defined in the SAP PP routing, recorded in minutes. Baseline for labor efficiency variance analysis and standard cost calculation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP/CO — Routing Planned Labor Time; IATF 16949 Clause 8.5.1

description: The timestamp when this production confirmation record was last modified in the source system. Tracks reversals, corrections, and status updates. Used for incremental data loading in the Databricks Silver Layer pipeline and change data capture (CDC) processing.
reference: SAP S/4HANA — Record Change Timestamp; Databricks Delta Lake CDC; IATF 16949 Clause 7.5

description: Actual machine runtime consumed for this production operation, recorded in minutes. Maps to SAP PP activity type for machine time (ISRÜT — Actual Machine Time). Core input for OEE calculation, takt time variance analysis, and capacity utilization reporting. Compared against planned machine time from the routing to identify bottlenecks.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP — Actual Machine Time (ISRÜT); IATF 16949 Clause 7.1.3; OEE Standard

description: Planned machine time for this operation as defined in the SAP PP routing, recorded in minutes. Baseline for actual vs. planned machine time variance analysis and takt time adherence reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP — Routing Planned Machine Time; IATF 16949 Clause 8.5.1

description: SAP material number (MATNR) of the finished or semi-finished product being confirmed. Identifies the vehicle model, sub-assembly, or component produced in this confirmation. Essential for inventory goods receipt posting and BOM (Bill of Materials) reconciliation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM/PP — Material Master (MATNR); ISO 9001 Clause 8.5.1

description: The confirmation or transaction reference number from the Apriso/Dassault MES system that corresponds to this SAP PP production confirmation. Enables cross-system traceability between the MES shop floor execution record and the SAP ERP confirmation posting. Critical for audit trails and discrepancy resolution.
reference: Apriso/Dassault MES — Shop Floor Execution Traceability; IATF 16949 Clause 8.5.2

description: The vehicle model year (MY) of the product being confirmed. Used for production output reporting by model year, regulatory compliance tracking (CAFE, EPA emissions), and alignment with SOP (Start of Production) and EOP (End of Production) milestones. Retained as a denormalized attribute for direct reporting without joining to vehicle configuration.
reference: NHTSA 49 CFR Part 565 — Model Year; CAFE Standards; EPA Emissions Reporting

description: Routing operation number (VORNR) within the production order being confirmed. Identifies the specific manufacturing step (e.g., 0010 = Stamping, 0020 = Body Weld, 0030 = Paint, 0040 = Final Assembly) for partial confirmation scenarios. Maps to SAP PP routing operation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP — Routing Operation (VORNR); Apriso/Dassault MES Operation Sequence

description: Status of the SAP CO production order settlement triggered by this final confirmation. 'pending' = settlement not yet executed; 'settled' = actual costs fully settled to cost objects; 'partially_settled' = partial settlement completed; 'not_required' = non-final confirmation, settlement not applicable. Used for period-end closing and financial reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-PC — Order Settlement (KO88); IATF 16949 Clause 9.1.3

description: The accounting/inventory posting date (SAP BUDAT) on which the production confirmation is financially posted. May differ from the confirmation timestamp when confirmations are entered retroactively. Drives inventory valuation, cost object settlement, and financial period assignment.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP/CO — Posting Date (BUDAT); IATF 16949 Clause 8.5.1

description: The powertrain technology type of the vehicle or assembly being confirmed. ICE = Internal Combustion Engine, HEV = Hybrid Electric Vehicle, PHEV = Plug-in Hybrid Electric Vehicle, BEV = Battery Electric Vehicle, FCEV = Fuel Cell Electric Vehicle. Used for regulatory reporting (CAFE, EPA, CARB), EV production mix analysis, and R&D investment tracking.
reference: EPA Clean Air Act — Powertrain Classification; CARB ZEV Mandate; CAFE Standards; UNECE Regulations

description: Flag indicating whether this confirmation record is a reversal entry (CO13 reversal in SAP PP). True = this record reverses a previously posted confirmation. Used to identify correction entries in production output reporting and to exclude reversed confirmations from yield and time aggregations.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP — CO13 Confirmation Reversal (STOKZ); IATF 16949 Clause 8.5.1

description: The quantity of units requiring rework (reprocessing to meet specification) identified during this confirmation. Distinct from scrap (which is permanently rejected). Used for quality cost analysis, rework labor tracking, and process capability assessment per APQP (Advanced Product Quality Planning) requirements.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM — Rework Quantity; IATF 16949 Clause 8.7.1; APQP

description: The quantity of units scrapped (rejected as non-conforming) during this production operation. Maps to SAP PP field XMNGA (Scrap Quantity). Used for PPM (Parts Per Million) defect rate calculation, scrap cost reporting, and FMEA (Failure Mode and Effects Analysis) input. Triggers quality notification creation when thresholds are exceeded.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP/QM — Scrap Quantity (XMNGA); IATF 16949 Clause 8.7 Nonconforming Outputs; SPC

description: Coded reason for scrap recorded against this confirmation. Maps to SAP QM defect codes or MES scrap reason catalog (e.g., 'DIM' = Dimensional Non-conformance, 'WLD' = Weld Defect, 'PNT' = Paint Defect, 'ASY' = Assembly Error). Required for PPM (Parts Per Million) defect analysis, FMEA updates, and quality improvement initiatives per IATF 16949.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM — Defect Code (FEHLERNR); IATF 16949 Clause 8.7.1; APQP Control Plan

description: Actual machine/workstation setup time consumed for this operation, recorded in minutes. Maps to SAP PP activity type for setup (ISRST — Actual Setup Time). Used for capacity planning, OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness) calculation, and comparison against planned setup time from the routing.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP — Actual Setup Time (ISRST); SAE J4000 Lean Manufacturing Assessment

description: Planned setup time for this operation as defined in the SAP PP routing, recorded in minutes. Provides the baseline for actual vs. planned setup time variance analysis. Sourced from the routing master data at the time of production order creation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP — Routing Planned Setup Time; IATF 16949 Clause 8.5.1

description: Actual takt time achieved for this production confirmation, expressed in seconds per unit. Calculated as total operation time divided by yield quantity. Used for production line balancing, bottleneck identification, and comparison against the planned takt time to assess line efficiency. Key metric in JIS (Just-in-Sequence) production management.
reference: SAE J4000 Lean Manufacturing; IATF 16949 Clause 8.5.1; Apriso MES Takt Time Monitoring

description: Actual teardown/cleanup time consumed after the production operation, recorded in minutes. Maps to SAP PP activity type for teardown. Included in total operation time for accurate takt time and capacity utilization calculations. Relevant for paint booth cleaning, tooling changeover, and AGV (Automated Guided Vehicle) repositioning cycles.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP — Actual Teardown Time; SAE J4000

description: 17-character Vehicle Identification Number assigned to the vehicle unit confirmed in this production record. Populated for final assembly confirmations where a specific vehicle unit is produced. Null for sub-assembly or component-level confirmations. Enables full build traceability from production confirmation to vehicle delivery.
reference: NHTSA 49 CFR Part 565 — VIN Standard; ISO 3779 Vehicle Identification Number; IATF 16949 Clause 8.5.2

description: The confirmed good quantity (yield) produced in this confirmation, expressed in the base unit of measure of the material. Represents the number of units that passed quality inspection and are accepted into inventory. Maps to SAP PP field LMNGA (Yield Quantity). Core metric for production output reporting and OTD (On-Time Delivery) tracking.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP — Yield Quantity (LMNGA); IATF 16949 Clause 9.1.1 Monitoring and Measurement

description: Primary key for production_order

Foreign key linking to billing.account. Business justification: Fleet and direct-customer production orders are tied to a billing account for invoice generation and credit management. Automotive OEM fleet order management requires linking the production order to t

Reference to the vehicle configuration or variant being built under this production order. Links to the vehicle configuration master (model, trim, powertrain, options) managed in Siemens Teamcenter PLM or SAP Variant Configuration.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM; SAP S/4HANA PP Variant Configuration; IATF 16949 Clause 8.3

description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Order cost tracking and variance analysis require linking each production order to its finance cost center, replacing the denormalized code.

Foreign key linking to customer.party. Business justification: Order‑to‑customer linkage required for order fulfillment, warranty, and recall reports; manufacturers need to know which party placed each production order.

Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Production order cost settlement and variance reporting are tied to fiscal periods for period-end closing. Automotive OEMs run monthly production cost variance reports by fiscal period; the fiscal_per

Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Production order cost posting uses a specific GL account; the order‑to‑GL link is required for the Production Order Cost Ledger report.

Foreign key linking to compliance.homologation_record. Business justification: Production Order release process checks Homologation_Record to ensure model/year has regulatory approval before manufacturing starts.
Reference to the manufacturing plant where this production order is executed. Corresponds to the SAP Plant (WERKS) organizational unit. Links to the plant master data product.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP/MM Module; IATF 16949 Clause 7.1

description: Reference to the Bill of Materials (BOM) version used for this production order. The BOM defines all components and sub-assemblies required to build the target material. Managed in SAP PP and Siemens Teamcenter PLM.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module; Siemens Teamcenter PLM; IATF 16949 Clause 8.3

description: Reference to the specific production line (e.g., stamping, body shop, paint, final assembly) assigned to execute this order. Maps to SAP Work Center (ARBPL) or MES line definition.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module; Apriso/Dassault MES; IATF 16949 Clause 8.5

description: Foreign key linking to manufacturing.production_schedule. Business justification: Production orders are released against a master production schedule (MPS). Adding production_schedule_id FK to production_order enables schedule adherence tracking — comparing planned quantities from
Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Order‑level profitability analysis (Order Profitability Statement) needs a profit_center FK to allocate revenue and cost.

Foreign key linking to finance.project. Business justification: New model launch and pilot-series production orders are linked to finance projects for CAPEX tracking and project cost controlling. Automotive OEMs track pre-series and SOP ramp-up production costs ag

Foreign key linking to supply.supply_purchase_order. Business justification: Production scheduling aligns each production order with its originating purchase order to ensure material availability and OTD compliance.

Reference to the production routing (task list / operation sequence) assigned to this order. The routing defines the sequence of manufacturing operations, work centers, and standard times. Maps to SAP PP Routing (PLNNR).
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module; IATF 16949 Clause 8.5

description: Reference to the production shift (e.g., Day, Afternoon, Night) assigned to execute this order. Used for capacity planning, labor allocation, and shift-level production reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module; Apriso/Dassault MES

description: Reference to the SAP Material Master (MATNR) representing the finished vehicle, sub-assembly, or component being produced. The primary product/material being manufactured under this order.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM/PP Module; IATF 16949 Clause 8.4

description: Foreign key linking to engineering.vehicle_program. Business justification: Each production order is created for a specific vehicle program; order tracking dashboards filter by vehicle_program_id.

Foreign key linking to finance.wbs_element. Business justification: Capital-intensive production orders (SOP pilot builds, EV platform launches) are settled to WBS elements in SAP PS for project cost controlling. Automotive OEMs require production order-to-WBS linkage

The total actual cost incurred to execute this production order, including material consumption, labor, machine, and overhead costs as settled in SAP CO. Expressed in the plant's local currency. Used for cost variance analysis, profitability reporting, and CapEx/OpEx tracking.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO Module; IATF 16949 Clause 9.1; GAAP/IFRS

description: The precise date and time when all production activities for this order were completed and the final goods receipt was posted, as recorded by MES or SAP PP. Used for cycle time calculation, OTD (On-Time Delivery) measurement, and throughput analysis.
reference: Apriso/Dassault MES; SAP S/4HANA PP Module; IATF 16949 Clause 8.5

description: The total actual direct labor hours consumed during execution of this production order, as confirmed through SAP PP operation confirmations or MES time tracking. Used for labor efficiency analysis, variance reporting, and cost actuals in SAP CO.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP/CO Module; Apriso/Dassault MES; IATF 16949 Clause 8.5

description: The total actual machine/equipment hours consumed during execution of this production order, as recorded by SCADA (Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition) systems or MES. Used for OEE analysis, machine utilization reporting, and cost variance calculation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP/CO Module; Apriso/Dassault MES; SCADA; IATF 16949 Clause 8.5

description: The precise date and time when production activities for this order actually commenced on the shop floor, as recorded by the MES or confirmed by the first operation confirmation in SAP PP. Used for cycle time analysis and schedule adherence measurement.
reference: Apriso/Dassault MES; SAP S/4HANA PP Module; IATF 16949 Clause 8.5

description: The Just-in-Sequence (JIS) sequence number assigned to this production order, defining its position in the assembly line build sequence. Critical for JIS supply chain synchronization — ensures parts arrive at the line in the exact order vehicles are built. Managed by MES and communicated to tier-1 suppliers.

The quantity of vehicles or sub-assemblies that have been confirmed as successfully produced and goods-receipted against this order. Maps to SAP PP confirmed yield quantity (WEMNG). Used for production performance and yield tracking.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module; Apriso/Dassault MES; IATF 16949 Clause 8.5

description: The date and time when this production order record was first created in the source system (SAP PP). Used for audit trail, data lineage, and record lifecycle management. Maps to SAP order creation timestamp (ERDAT + ERZET).
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module; IATF 16949 Clause 7.5; ISO 9001 Clause 7.5

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the standard_cost and actual_cost amounts on this production order (e.g., USD, EUR, JPY, MXN). Determined by the plant's controlling area currency in SAP CO.

Indicates whether this production order involves the use or production of hazardous materials (e.g., battery electrolytes for EV/HEV powertrains, paint solvents, chemical treatments). True = hazardous materials involved, requiring compliance with EPA, CARB, and ISO 14001 environmental management protocols.

The date and time when this production order record was most recently modified in the source system. Used for change tracking, delta load processing in the lakehouse pipeline, and audit compliance under IATF 16949.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module; IATF 16949 Clause 7.5; ISO 9001 Clause 7.5

description: Cross-reference identifier assigned by the Apriso/Dassault MES system for this production order. Used to correlate SAP PP orders with shop floor execution records in the MES for traceability and real-time production control.

The vehicle model year (MY) associated with the units being produced under this order. Critical for regulatory compliance (CAFE, EPA, NCAP), warranty tracking, and production program management. Typically a 4-digit year (e.g., 2025).
reference: NHTSA; EPA; CAFE Standards; SAE International

description: The business date on which the production order was created and issued in SAP PP. This is the principal business event date representing when the manufacturing work order was formally raised. Maps to SAP order creation date (ERDAT).
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module; IATF 16949 Clause 8.5

description: Free-text description of the production order providing additional context about the manufacturing scope, special instructions, or engineering change notes. Used by shop floor supervisors and production planners for operational clarity.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module; Apriso/Dassault MES

description: Externally-known alphanumeric identifier for the production order as assigned by SAP PP (Production Planning) or Apriso MES. This is the business-facing reference used on the shop floor, in MES screens, and in cross-system communication. Maps to SAP PP order number (AUFNR).
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module; IATF 16949 Clause 8.5

description: Current lifecycle state of the production order within the SAP PP and MES workflow. 'created' = order created but not yet released to shop floor; 'released' = released for execution; 'in_progress' = active manufacturing underway; 'partially_confirmed' = some operations confirmed; 'confirmed' = all operations confirmed; 'technically_completed' = TECO status in SAP — production complete, pending settlement; 'closed' = fully settled and archived; 'cancelled' = order voided. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: created|released|in_progress|partially_confirmed|confirmed|technically_completed|closed|cancelled — promote to reference product]
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module; IATF 16949 Clause 8.5

description: Classification of the production order indicating its manufacturing purpose. 'vehicle_build' represents a full vehicle assembly order; 'sub_assembly' covers component or module builds (e.g., powertrain, axle); 'rework' covers defect correction orders; 'repair' covers warranty or field return repairs; 'prototype' covers R&D and pre-SOP (Start of Production) builds. Maps to SAP PP order type (AUART).
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module; IATF 16949 Clause 8.5

description: The scheduled date on which all production activities for this order are planned to be completed. Used for delivery commitment, JIT (Just-in-Time) scheduling, and OTD (On-Time Delivery) performance tracking. Maps to SAP PP planned finish date (GLTRS).
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module; IATF 16949 Clause 8.5

description: The total planned direct labor hours required to execute this production order, derived from the routing standard times and target quantity. Used for capacity planning, workforce scheduling via SuccessFactors, and standard cost calculation in SAP CO.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP/CO Module; SuccessFactors; IATF 16949 Clause 7.1

description: The total planned machine/equipment hours required to execute this production order, derived from routing standard machine times. Used for equipment capacity planning, OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness) baseline, and standard cost calculation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP/CO Module; IATF 16949 Clause 7.1

description: The scheduled date on which production activities for this order are planned to begin, as determined by MRP (Material Requirements Planning) or production scheduling. Maps to SAP PP planned start date (GSTRS).
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP/MRP Module; IATF 16949 Clause 8.5

business_glossary_term: Production Part Approval Process (PPAP) Required Flag
description: Indicates whether PPAP (Production Part Approval Process) approval is required before this production order can be released for full-volume production. Typically True for new part introductions, engineering changes, or supplier changes. Governed by IATF 16949 and AIAG PPAP standards.

Numeric priority level assigned to this production order for shop floor scheduling and sequencing. Lower values indicate higher priority. Used by MES and JIS (Just-in-Sequence) systems to determine execution order when multiple orders compete for the same resources.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module; Apriso/Dassault MES; JIS

description: The manufacturing stage or shop area where this production order is executed. Reflects the physical production process: 'stamping' = metal forming; 'body_shop' = body-in-white welding; 'paint' = surface coating; 'final_assembly' = vehicle assembly; 'powertrain' = engine/transmission build; 'trim_chassis' = trim, chassis, and final fit.
reference: Apriso/Dassault MES; IATF 16949 Clause 8.5; SAE International

description: The date and time when the production order was formally released to the shop floor for execution. Release triggers material staging, capacity reservation, and MES order activation. Maps to SAP PP order release date/time.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module; Apriso/Dassault MES; IATF 16949 Clause 8.5

description: The quantity of units that required rework (non-conforming units corrected and returned to production flow) during execution of this order. Distinct from scrap — reworked units are salvaged. Used for quality cost analysis and APQP (Advanced Product Quality Planning) reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM Module; IATF 16949 Clause 8.7; APQP

description: The quantity of units scrapped or rejected during production of this order due to quality defects or process failures. Used for PPM (Parts Per Million) defect rate calculation, scrap cost analysis, and FMEA (Failure Mode and Effects Analysis) reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP/QM Module; IATF 16949 Clause 8.7; SPC

description: Indicates whether this production order is part of the SOP (Start of Production) launch phase for a new vehicle program. True = SOP launch order subject to enhanced quality monitoring and APQP controls; False = regular series production order.

The planned standard cost to produce the target quantity under this order, calculated from the BOM (Bill of Materials) component costs and routing activity rates in SAP CO (Controlling). Expressed in the plant's local currency. Used for cost variance analysis and EBITDA reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO Module; IATF 16949 Clause 9.1; GAAP/IFRS

description: The planned quantity of vehicles or sub-assemblies to be produced under this order, expressed in the base unit of measure (typically 'EA' for each). Maps to SAP PP planned order quantity (GAMNG). Used for capacity planning and production scheduling.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module; IATF 16949 Clause 8.5

description: The date and time when the production order was set to Technically Completed (TECO) status in SAP PP, indicating all manufacturing operations are finished and the order is pending cost settlement. Distinct from actual_finish_timestamp which records the last physical operation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP/CO Module; IATF 16949 Clause 8.5

description: The base unit of measure for the target and confirmed quantities on this production order (e.g., EA = Each for vehicles, PC = Piece for components, SET for kits, KG for bulk materials). Maps to SAP base unit of measure (MEINS).

The last VIN (Vehicle Identification Number) in the sequential range assigned to units produced under this order. Together with vin_range_start, defines the complete set of vehicles traceable to this production order for recall, warranty, and homologation purposes.
reference: ISO 3779; NHTSA 49 CFR Part 565; SAE J272

description: The first VIN (Vehicle Identification Number) in the sequential range assigned to units produced under this order. VINs are 17-character alphanumeric identifiers conforming to ISO 3779. Used for build traceability, recall management, and regulatory reporting to NHTSA.
reference: ISO 3779; NHTSA 49 CFR Part 565; SAE J272

description: Indicates whether WIP (Work in Progress) costs have been calculated and posted to the financial ledger for this production order in the current period. True = WIP posted; False = WIP not yet posted. Used for period-end financial closing in SAP CO.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO Module; GAAP/IFRS IAS 2

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the rework order record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Primary key for this entity.
reference: IATF 16949 Section 8.7 - Control of Nonconforming Outputs

description: Foreign key linking to manufacturing.build_stage. Business justification: Rework orders are initiated at a specific build stage where the defect was detected or where rework is performed. The assembly_stage_code STRING is a denormalized reference to the build_stage master.

Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Rework labor and material costs must be posted to a cost center for quality cost reporting, warranty reserve calculations, and IATF 16949 quality cost analysis. Automotive quality controllers require
Reference to the quality defect record in the quality domain that triggered this rework order. Links rework traceability to the originating inspection finding or defect notification.
reference: IATF 16949 Section 8.7; SAP QM Defect Record

description: Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Rework costs (labor and material) are posted to specific GL accounts (quality cost expense, scrap/rework accounts) for P&L reporting and cost-of-poor-quality analysis. Automotive finance requires GL-l

Foreign key linking to supply.inbound_part. Business justification: Rework orders caused by defective inbound parts require traceability to the specific supplier part for SCAR issuance, PPAP re-submission triggers, and warranty cost recovery from suppliers. This is a

Foreign key linking to quality.inspection_lot. Business justification: After rework completion, a re-inspection lot is created to verify rework effectiveness. re_inspection_required field on rework_order indicates this need. Linking to inspection_lot enables rework verif
Reference to the manufacturing plant where the rework operation is being executed. Aligns with SAP Plant (Werk) organizational unit.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Organizational Structure; IATF 16949 Section 4.1

description: Reference to the originating production order from which this rework order was raised. Links the rework event back to the scheduled manufacturing work order in SAP PP or MES.
reference: SAP PP Production Order; IATF 16949 Section 8.5.1

description: Foreign key linking to inventory.sku_master. Business justification: Rework orders consume replacement parts that must be issued from inventory and tracked against specific SKUs for cost accounting, material replenishment triggering, and IATF 16949 quality cost reporti

Foreign key linking to manufacturing.vehicle_build. Business justification: A rework_order is initiated on a specific vehicle_build that failed quality inspection. The vin STRING column is a denormalized vehicle identifier; replacing it with vehicle_build_id FK provides direc
Reference to the work center or repair station assigned to execute the rework operation. Corresponds to SAP PP Work Center or MES work station definition.
reference: SAP PP Work Center; Apriso/Dassault MES Work Station

description: Actual date and time when the rework operation was completed and the unit was released for re-inspection or returned to the production line. Recorded by MES upon order closure.
reference: Apriso/Dassault MES Time Tracking; SAP PM Order Confirmation

description: Actual labor hours consumed to complete the rework operation, recorded upon order completion in MES or SAP PM. Compared against planned hours for efficiency analysis and cost variance reporting.
reference: SAP PM Actual Costs; Apriso/Dassault MES Time Tracking; IATF 16949 Section 9.1

description: Actual date and time when the rework technician began executing the rework operation, as recorded by MES shop floor data collection or operator confirmation.
reference: Apriso/Dassault MES Time Tracking; SAP PM Order Confirmation

description: The BOM (Bill of Materials) component or part number of the specific sub-assembly or component requiring rework. References the engineering BOM managed in Siemens Teamcenter or SAP MM.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM BOM Management; SAP MM Material Master; IATF 16949 Section 8.5.1

description: Reference number or identifier of the corrective action (CAR) or 8D problem-solving report raised in response to this rework event. Links the rework order to the formal quality improvement process.
reference: IATF 16949 Section 10.2.1 - Corrective Action; SAP QM Corrective Action; APQP 8D Report

description: Date and time when the rework order record was first created in the source system (MES or SAP QM). Audit trail field for data lineage and compliance purposes.
reference: IATF 16949 Section 7.5 - Documented Information; ISO 9001:2015 Section 7.5

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary cost fields on this rework order (rework_labor_cost, rework_material_cost). Typically the plant's local operating currency (e.g., USD, EUR, MXN).
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; SAP FI Currency Configuration

description: Standardized defect classification code identifying the type of nonconformance that triggered the rework order. Aligns with the plant defect code catalogue maintained in SAP QM or MES quality module.
reference: IATF 16949 Section 8.7; SAP QM Defect Code Catalogue

description: Free-text narrative description of the defect or nonconformance identified during in-process quality inspection that initiated this rework order. Captured by the quality inspector or MES operator.
reference: IATF 16949 Section 8.7; SAP QM Notification Long Text

description: Standardized code identifying the physical location or zone on the vehicle or sub-assembly where the defect was found (e.g., body zone, paint zone, chassis area). Used for spatial defect analysis and NVH (Noise Vibration Harshness) correlation.
reference: IATF 16949 Section 8.7; Euro NCAP Defect Zone Classification

description: Code indicating the method by which the defect was detected prior to rework order creation (e.g., visual inspection, automated gauge check, functional test, quality audit, customer feedback). Supports FMEA (Failure Mode and Effects Analysis) detection ranking analysis.
reference: IATF 16949 Section 8.6; FMEA Detection Ranking; SAP QM Inspection Method

description: Date and time when the nonconformance was originally detected during in-process quality inspection, triggering the creation of this rework order. The principal business event timestamp for this transaction.
reference: IATF 16949 Section 8.6; SAP QM Inspection Lot Creation Date; Apriso MES Quality Event

description: Final disposition decision for the nonconforming unit after rework evaluation. Repaired indicates successful correction; scrapped indicates the unit is unrecoverable; concession (use-as-is) indicates acceptance of the nonconformance with documented deviation; return to supplier applies to purchased components.
reference: IATF 16949 Section 8.7.1 - Disposition of Nonconforming Product; ISO 9001:2015 Section 8.7

description: Code identifying the specific quality inspection checkpoint or gate (e.g., body-in-white audit, paint final audit, end-of-line check) at which the defect was detected and the rework order was initiated.
reference: IATF 16949 Section 8.6; SAP QM Inspection Lot; Apriso MES Quality Gate

description: Indicates whether the vehicle or sub-assembly is placed on a regulatory hold pending rework completion and re-inspection. A regulatory hold prevents the unit from advancing to the next production stage or shipping.
reference: NHTSA 49 CFR Part 573; CARB Compliance; DOT FMVSS; IATF 16949 Section 8.7

description: Indicates whether the defect triggering this rework order is classified as safety-critical, requiring escalation per NHTSA, DOT, or internal safety reporting protocols. Safety-related rework orders may trigger FMEA review and potential field action assessment.
reference: NHTSA 49 CFR Part 573 - Defect and Noncompliance Reports; DOT FMVSS; IATF 16949 Section 9.1.3

description: Indicates whether this rework event caused a production line stoppage (Andon pull or line stop). Used for OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness) impact analysis and production downtime reporting.
reference: IATF 16949 Section 8.5.1; Apriso/Dassault MES Andon Event; SAP PP Downtime Recording

description: The vehicle model year (MY) of the unit undergoing rework. Used for regulatory compliance reporting, warranty analysis, and production quality trending by model year.
reference: NHTSA 49 CFR Part 565; SAP PP Production Version

description: Scheduled date and time when the rework operation is planned to be completed. Used to calculate planned rework cycle time and assess impact on downstream production sequencing.
reference: Apriso/Dassault MES Scheduling; SAP PP Scheduling

description: Estimated labor hours planned to complete the rework operation, as defined in the rework routing or standard repair time (SRT) catalogue. Used for capacity planning and cost estimation.
reference: SAP PP Routing Standard Values; IATF 16949 Section 8.5.1

description: Scheduled date and time when the rework operation is planned to begin, as determined by the MES scheduler or production planner. Used for shop floor capacity planning and JIS (Just-in-Sequence) scheduling.
reference: Apriso/Dassault MES Scheduling; SAP PP Scheduling

description: Business priority assigned to the rework order to drive scheduling and resource allocation on the shop floor. Critical priority typically indicates a safety-related or regulatory nonconformance requiring immediate resolution.
reference: IATF 16949 Section 8.7; SAP QM Notification Priority

description: Code identifying the specific production line (e.g., stamping, body shop, paint, final assembly) where the defect was originally detected and the rework order was raised.
reference: Apriso/Dassault MES Production Line Configuration; SAP PP Work Center Hierarchy

description: Indicates whether a formal re-inspection is required after rework completion before the unit can be released to the next production stage or shipped. Mandatory for safety-related or regulatory nonconformances.
reference: IATF 16949 Section 8.7.1; SAP QM Re-Inspection Trigger

description: Outcome of the quality re-inspection performed after rework completion. Pass indicates the unit meets specification; fail triggers a secondary rework or scrap decision; conditional pass indicates acceptance with documented deviation.
reference: IATF 16949 Section 8.6; SAP QM Usage Decision; Apriso MES Quality Gate Result

description: Number of rework attempts made on this unit or component for the same defect. Multiple rework attempts on the same unit indicate a chronic quality issue and may trigger escalation or scrap decision per quality policy.
reference: IATF 16949 Section 8.7.1; SAP QM Notification Recurrence

description: Document number or reference to the approved rework instruction, repair standard, or Technical Service Bulletin (TSB) that governs the rework procedure. Ensures traceability to authorized repair methods.
reference: IATF 16949 Section 8.5.1; SAP DMS Document Reference; Siemens Teamcenter Document Management

description: Total internal labor cost incurred for the rework operation, calculated from actual rework hours and the applicable labor rate. Expressed in the plant's operating currency. Used for quality cost (Cost of Poor Quality - COPQ) reporting.
reference: SAP CO Cost Object Controlling; IATF 16949 Section 9.1; APQP Cost of Poor Quality

description: Cost of replacement parts and materials consumed during the rework operation. Sourced from SAP MM goods issue postings against the rework order. Contributes to total Cost of Poor Quality (COPQ) calculation.
reference: SAP MM Goods Issue; SAP CO Cost Object; IATF 16949 Section 9.1

description: Externally-known, human-readable business identifier for the rework order, typically generated by the MES (Manufacturing Execution System) or SAP QM module. Format: RWK-{PlantCode}-{Year}-{Sequence}.
reference: IATF 16949 Section 8.7; SAP QM Notification Number

description: Current lifecycle status of the rework order within the shop floor execution workflow. Tracks progression from creation through completion or cancellation as managed in MES or SAP QM.
reference: IATF 16949 Section 8.7; Apriso/Dassault MES Order Status

description: Classification of the rework operation method. Inline repair is performed on the moving assembly line; offline rework removes the unit from the line; teardown involves partial or full disassembly; subassembly rework targets a component before vehicle integration; cosmetic repair addresses surface or finish defects. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: inline_repair|offline_rework|teardown|subassembly_rework|cosmetic_repair|field_return_rework — promote to reference product]
reference: IATF 16949 Section 8.7.1; APQP Phase 4 - Product and Process Validation

description: Standardized code classifying the root cause of the defect as determined through root cause analysis (e.g., 5-Why, Ishikawa). Populated upon rework completion. Supports FMEA (Failure Mode and Effects Analysis) and SPC (Statistical Process Control) improvement actions.
reference: IATF 16949 Section 10.2; FMEA Methodology; SAP QM Cause Code

description: Number of components or sub-assemblies scrapped as a result of this rework order where repair was not feasible. Used for PPM (Parts Per Million) defect rate calculation and scrap cost reporting.
reference: IATF 16949 Section 8.7; SAP PP Scrap Posting; PPM Defect Rate Reporting

description: Code identifying the operational system of record from which this rework order record was sourced (e.g., SAP QM, Apriso/Dassault MES, or manual entry). Supports data lineage and Silver Layer ingestion traceability.
reference: Enterprise Data Governance - Data Lineage Standard; Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer Conventions

description: Date and time when the rework order record was last modified in the source system. Supports incremental data loading in the Databricks Silver Layer and audit trail requirements.
reference: IATF 16949 Section 7.5 - Documented Information; ISO 9001:2015 Section 7.5

description: Internal product/model code identifying the vehicle nameplate or platform (e.g., truck, SUV, EV, HEV) associated with the rework order. Aligns with PLM product structure in Siemens Teamcenter.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM Product Structure; SAP MM Material Master

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each shop floor event record generated by MES, PLC, or SCADA systems during production operations.

Foreign key linking to manufacturing.build_stage. Business justification: Shop floor events (MES/PLC/SCADA) occur at specific build stages (stamping, body shop, paint, final assembly). Linking shop_floor_event to build_stage enables stage-level OEE analysis, quality gate ev

Foreign key linking to quality.characteristic. Business justification: Shop floor events capture measured_value against control limits — these measurements are for specific quality characteristics. Linking to characteristic enables proper SPC data traceability and charac

Foreign key linking to quality.defect_record. Business justification: IATF 16949 MES traceability: shop floor events with quality_result='FAIL' or fault_code directly generate defect records. Linking enables MES-to-quality defect traceability required for process audit

Foreign key linking to quality.inspection_lot. Business justification: Quality gate shop floor events trigger inspection lots. MES quality gate events must reference the resulting inspection lot for in-process quality disposition traceability per IATF 16949 unit-level tr
Reference to the manufacturing plant where the event occurred. Supports multi-plant OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness) analysis and regulatory reporting.

Foreign key linking to manufacturing.production_line. Business justification: Shop floor events are generated on specific production lines. The production_line_code STRING is a denormalized reference. Adding production_line_id FK enables line-level event aggregation for OEE cal
Reference to the SAP PP production order under which this shop floor event was generated. Links the event to the scheduled manufacturing work order.
Reference to the production shift during which this event was recorded. Enables shift-level OEE and throughput analysis.
Reference to the vehicle unit being processed at the time of this event. Enables full build traceability from stamping through final assembly per NHTSA VIN requirements.
Reference to the SAP PP/MES work center (station, cell, or line segment) where this event occurred. Identifies the physical production resource.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP – Work Center; IATF 16949

description: Identifier of the AGV (Automated Guided Vehicle) unit involved in this event. Populated only for AGV arrival, departure, or fault events. Enables AGV fleet utilization and route efficiency analysis.
reference: Apriso/Dassault MES – AGV Integration; ISO 3691-4 – AGV Safety

description: Major manufacturing area where the event occurred. Supports area-level OEE reporting and quality gate management across stamping, body shop, paint, and final assembly. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: stamping|body_shop|paint_shop|final_assembly|powertrain|chassis|trim — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: IATF 16949 – Process Control Plan; Apriso MES

description: Timestamp when this shop floor event record was first ingested into the Databricks Silver layer. Distinct from event_timestamp (real-world event time). Used for data pipeline latency monitoring and audit trail.
High-level grouping of the event type for reporting and analytics. Enables aggregated OEE loss categorization (availability, performance, quality) and safety incident tracking.

Duration of the shop floor event in seconds (e.g., station cycle time, weld duration, paint cure time). Principal quantitative measurement for cycle time analysis and OEE performance rate calculation.

Structured JSON string containing the full event-specific payload from the MES, PLC, or SCADA system. Stores additional context-specific parameters not captured in dedicated columns (e.g., weld schedule parameters, paint recipe ID, torque angle). Stored as STRING to comply with Silver layer no-complex-type constraint.
reference: Apriso/Dassault MES – Event Payload; ISA-95 Manufacturing Operations

description: Identifies the originating control system that generated this event (MES, PLC, SCADA, AGV controller, or manual entry). Critical for data lineage and root-cause analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: MES|PLC|SCADA|AGV|TPMS|OBD|MANUAL — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: Apriso/Dassault MES; IEC 61131 – PLC Standards; ISA-95

description: Current lifecycle state of the shop floor event record. Indicates whether the event has been acknowledged, confirmed by the operator, placed on hold, or escalated for quality review.

Precise date and time (ISO 8601 with timezone offset) when the shop floor event occurred as recorded by the MES, PLC, or SCADA system. This is the principal real-world event time used for OEE calculation and production sequencing.

Discriminator classifying the kind of shop floor event. Drives downstream routing, OEE calculation, and quality gate logic. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: station_entry|station_exit|torque_confirmation|weld_completion|paint_cure|agv_arrival|hold_trigger|alarm|inspection_pass|inspection_fail|rework_start|rework_complete — promote to reference product]

Machine or process fault code generated by the PLC or SCADA system when an alarm or hold event occurs. Used for root-cause analysis, FMEA (Failure Mode and Effects Analysis) updates, and maintenance work order creation.
reference: IEC 61131 – PLC Fault Codes; IATF 16949 FMEA; SAP PM

description: Human-readable description of the fault or alarm condition associated with this event. Populated from the PLC/SCADA alarm dictionary. Null for non-alarm events.

Coded reason for a production hold triggered by this event (e.g., QUAL-FAIL, TORQUE-OOC, WELD-MISS, PART-MISSING). Populated only for hold_trigger event types. Drives quality containment and corrective action workflows.
reference: IATF 16949 – Nonconforming Product Control; SAP QM

description: Indicates whether this event is associated with a rework operation (True) rather than a first-pass production operation (False). Used to calculate first-time quality (FTQ) and rework cost metrics.

Indicates whether the measured value falls within the defined process control limits (True) or represents an out-of-specification condition (False). Drives automatic quality hold triggers and PPM (Parts Per Million) defect rate calculation.
reference: IATF 16949 – Process Control; AIAG FMEA Manual

description: Lower statistical process control limit for the measured value at this station. Used in SPC (Statistical Process Control) to detect process drift and trigger quality holds.
reference: IATF 16949 – SPC; AIAG SPC Manual 2nd Edition

description: Principal numeric measurement captured during the event (e.g., torque value in Nm, weld current in A, paint film thickness in µm, temperature in °C, pressure in bar). Null when the event type does not produce a measurement.

Engineering unit of measure for the measured_value field (e.g., Nm for torque, A for weld current, µm for paint film thickness). Ensures dimensional correctness in SPC and quality analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Nm|A|V|bar|°C|µm|mm|kg|kN|rpm|% — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Externally-known unique event reference number assigned by the Apriso/Dassault MES system. Used for cross-system traceability and audit correlation.
Four-digit model year (MY) of the vehicle being produced at the time of this event. Supports model-year-level production analytics and regulatory compliance reporting.
reference: NHTSA 49 CFR Part 565; SAP S/4HANA PP

description: Classifies this event's contribution to OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness) loss: availability loss (unplanned downtime), performance loss (speed reduction), quality loss (defects/rework), or no_loss (normal production). Enables direct OEE waterfall analysis per ISO 22400.
Standard planned cycle time in seconds for this process step as defined in the SAP PP routing. Compared against event_duration_seconds to calculate performance rate for OEE.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP – Routing Standard Values; ISO 22400-2 OEE

description: Powertrain technology type of the vehicle being produced (ICE – Internal Combustion Engine, HEV – Hybrid Electric Vehicle, PHEV – Plug-in Hybrid Electric Vehicle, BEV – Battery Electric Vehicle, FCEV – Fuel Cell Electric Vehicle). Drives process routing differences and regulatory compliance checks.
reference: SAE J1715 – Hybrid Vehicle Terminology; EPA Emissions Standards

description: Code identifying the specific manufacturing process step (e.g., STAMP-001, BODY-WELD-045, PAINT-CURE-010, ASSY-TORQUE-120) within the routing. Aligns with SAP PP routing operations.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP – Routing; IATF 16949 Process Control

description: Version-controlled program number loaded on the PLC or robot controller at the time of this event. Critical for traceability of process parameter changes and engineering change management.
reference: IEC 61131 – PLC Programming; Siemens Teamcenter PLM – Engineering Change

description: Outcome of the quality check associated with this event. Determines whether the vehicle unit proceeds to the next station, requires rework, or is scrapped. Feeds into IATF 16949 quality records and PPM reporting.
reference: IATF 16949 – Quality Gate; APQP (Advanced Product Quality Planning)

description: Sequential count of rework attempts for this vehicle unit at this station. A value greater than 1 indicates repeated failures and triggers escalation per IATF 16949 nonconforming product procedures.
Coded reason for scrapping a component or vehicle unit at this event. Populated only when quality_result is 'scrap'. Feeds into SAP CO scrap cost reporting and IATF 16949 quality records.
reference: IATF 16949 – Scrap Management; SAP S/4HANA CO

description: Production sequence number assigned to the vehicle unit in the JIS (Just-in-Sequence) build schedule. Used to validate correct part delivery sequencing and detect sequence deviations.
reference: Apriso/Dassault MES – JIS Sequencing; SAP S/4HANA PP

description: Unique code of the specific work station or assembly point within the production line where the event was triggered. Enables station-level cycle time and downtime analysis.
Human-readable descriptive name of the work station (e.g., 'Underbody Weld Station 3', 'E-Coat Oven Exit', 'Engine Dress Final'). Used in operator dashboards and quality reports.

Timestamp of the most recent update to this shop floor event record in the Silver layer (e.g., when quality result or hold status was updated post-event). Supports change data capture and audit compliance.
Upper statistical process control limit for the measured value at this station. Used in SPC (Statistical Process Control) to detect process drift and trigger quality holds.
reference: IATF 16949 – SPC; AIAG SPC Manual 2nd Edition

description: Internal code identifying the vehicle model or platform being assembled (e.g., F-150, RAV4, Model-3-LR). Enables model-mix production analysis and JIS (Just-in-Sequence) validation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP – Material Master; Siemens Teamcenter PLM

description: 17-character Vehicle Identification Number of the vehicle unit being processed. Denormalized here for direct event-level traceability without requiring a join. Conforms to ISO 3779 and NHTSA 49 CFR Part 565.
reference: NHTSA 49 CFR Part 565; ISO 3779 – VIN Structure

description: Primary key for vehicle_build

Foreign key linking to engineering.bom. Business justification: Vehicle build records must reference the exact engineering BOM revision used during assembly for quality traceability, recall investigations, and warranty analysis. Regulators and OEM quality systems

Foreign key linking to manufacturing.build_sequence. Business justification: A vehicle_build is executed against a specific build_sequence (JIS sequence entry). The build_sequence_number INT column is a denormalized reference to the sequence record. Replacing it with build_seq
Foreign key linking to manufacturing.build_stage. Business justification: Vehicle build references its current build stage; replace string column with FK to build_stage for normalization.

Foreign key linking to vehicle.configuration. Business justification: End-of-line quality validation requires confirming the physical vehicle_build matches the ordered configuration (options, trim, powertrain, market). This direct FK enables build-to-order conformance c

Foreign key linking to inventory.finished_vehicle_stock. Business justification: Vehicle build completion triggers creation of a finished vehicle stock record — this is the fundamental manufacturing-to-inventory handoff. Traceability from build record to finished goods stock is re
Foreign key linking to compliance.homologation_record. Business justification: Each built vehicle must be tied to its Homologation_Record for VIN‑level compliance verification and reporting.

Foreign key linking to quality.inspection_lot. Business justification: End-of-line and PDI inspection lots are created per vehicle build (per VIN). IATF 16949 requires unit-level quality disposition traceability from vehicle build to its inspection lot and final quality
Reference to the manufacturing plant where this vehicle was assembled. Enables plant-level production reporting and capacity analysis.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP – Plant / Work Center
