description: Foreign key linking to vehicle.powertrain_variant. Business justification: vehicle_build records engine_serial_number, transmission_serial_number, and battery_serial_number — physical powertrain components installed. Linking to powertrain_variant enables end-of-line powertra

Foreign key linking to manufacturing.production_line. Business justification: A vehicle_build occurs on a specific production line. The assembly_line_code STRING is a denormalized reference to the production_line master. Adding production_line_id FK enables line-level build thr
Reference to the SAP PP production order that authorized and scheduled this vehicle build. Links the build event to the manufacturing planning hierarchy.

Foreign key linking to manufacturing.production_variant. Business justification: Each vehicle_build produces a specific production variant. Adding production_variant_id FK links the actual build event to the variant master, enabling variant-level build quality analysis, takt time
Reference to the production shift during which this vehicle build was executed. Supports shift-level throughput and quality analysis.

Foreign key linking to sales.vehicle_order. Business justification: Enables Build ↔ Delivery scheduling; associates each built vehicle with the sales order it fulfills for handover and logistics planning.
Foreign key linking to vehicle.vin_registry. Business justification: VIN Registration Integration needed for warranty, recall, and regulatory reporting linking build record to VIN registry.

The unique serial number of the high-voltage traction battery pack installed in this vehicle (applicable to HEV, PHEV, BEV). Null for ICE vehicles. Critical for EV battery genealogy, warranty, and end-of-life regulatory compliance.
reference: IATF 16949 Clause 8.5.2; EU Battery Regulation 2023/1542; CARB ZEV Regulations

description: Actual timestamp when the body shop stage was completed and the body-in-white was released to the paint shop. Null if stage not yet completed.
reference: Apriso/Dassault MES – Stage Tracking; IATF 16949 Clause 8.5.1

description: Actual timestamp when the vehicle body-in-white (BIW) entered the body shop for welding and assembly. Key milestone for build genealogy and stage cycle time tracking.
reference: Apriso/Dassault MES – Stage Tracking; IATF 16949 Clause 8.5.1

description: The body style classification of the vehicle being built. Used for production line routing, tooling selection, and regulatory homologation grouping.
reference: SAE J1100 – Vehicle Body Style Classification; UNECE Regulation No. 55

description: The specific BOM (Bill of Materials) version or engineering change level used to build this vehicle. Captured at build time for genealogy and recall traceability. Links to Siemens Teamcenter PLM BOM management.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM – BOM Management; IATF 16949 Clause 8.5.2 – Traceability

description: Actual date and time when the vehicle build was completed at the final assembly stage and signed off for PDI (Pre-Delivery Inspection). Null if the build is still in progress. Used for cycle time, throughput, and SOP/EOP milestone tracking.
reference: Apriso/Dassault MES – Work Order Actual Finish; IATF 16949 Clause 8.5.1

description: Actual date and time when the vehicle build process commenced at the first production stage (typically body-in-white or stamping entry). Principal business event timestamp for the build lifecycle. Used for cycle time calculation and OTD (On-Time Delivery) analysis.
reference: Apriso/Dassault MES – Work Order Actual Start; IATF 16949 Clause 8.5.1

description: Current lifecycle status of the vehicle build record. Drives shop floor workflow, MES gating, and production reporting. 'on_hold' indicates a quality or material hold; 'rework' indicates a defect requiring corrective action before completion.
reference: Apriso/Dassault MES – Work Order Status; IATF 16949 Clause 8.5.2

description: Classification of the vehicle build type: standard (full local assembly), CKD (Completely Knocked Down – assembled from imported kits), SKD (Semi Knocked Down), pilot (pre-SOP trial build), or prototype (engineering validation). Affects BOM, duty classification, and regulatory reporting.
reference: SAP PP – Order Type; WTO Customs Valuation; IATF 16949 APQP

description: Actual timestamp when the chassis assembly stage was completed. Null if stage not yet completed.
reference: Apriso/Dassault MES – Stage Tracking; IATF 16949 Clause 8.5.1

description: Actual timestamp when the vehicle entered the chassis assembly stage for powertrain marriage, suspension, and drivetrain installation.
reference: Apriso/Dassault MES – Stage Tracking; IATF 16949 Clause 8.5.1

description: Timestamp when this vehicle build record was first created in the MES/data platform. Represents the audit trail creation event, distinct from the build_start_timestamp which is the actual shop floor event time.
reference: IATF 16949 Clause 7.5 – Documented Information; ISO 9001 Clause 7.5

description: Result of the automated end-of-line (EOL) functional test performed on the completed vehicle, covering powertrain, brakes, ADAS, and electrical systems. Distinct from quality_gate_status which covers visual/dimensional inspection.
reference: IATF 16949 Clause 8.6; DOT FMVSS – Federal Motor Vehicle Safety Standards; SAE J1739 FMEA

description: The unique serial number of the engine unit installed in this vehicle during chassis assembly. Critical for powertrain genealogy, warranty traceability, and recall management.

description: Actual timestamp when the final assembly stage was completed and the vehicle was released for PDI (Pre-Delivery Inspection). Null if stage not yet completed.

description: Actual timestamp when the vehicle entered the final assembly stage for fluids, wheels, glass, and end-of-line checks.
reference: Apriso/Dassault MES – Stage Tracking; IATF 16949 Clause 8.5.1

description: Indicates whether this vehicle build is currently under a production hold (True = on hold). A hold may be triggered by a quality non-conformance, missing parts, engineering change, or safety concern. Drives MES gating to prevent further assembly until resolved.

description: The manufacturer's paint color code applied to this vehicle during the paint stage (e.g., G1W – Super White, 040 – Classic White). Used for paint shop scheduling, color sequencing optimization, and customer order matching.

description: Actual timestamp when the paint stage was completed and the vehicle body was released to the trim/general assembly line. Null if stage not yet completed.
Actual timestamp when the vehicle body entered the paint shop for priming, painting, and clear-coat application.
reference: Apriso/Dassault MES – Stage Tracking; ISO 14001 – Environmental Management (paint VOC emissions)

description: Classification of the vehicle powertrain technology being assembled: ICE (Internal Combustion Engine), HEV (Hybrid Electric Vehicle), PHEV (Plug-in Hybrid Electric Vehicle), BEV (Battery Electric Vehicle), or FCEV (Fuel Cell Electric Vehicle). Drives assembly line routing, tooling, and EPA/CARB emissions compliance tracking.
reference: EPA 40 CFR Part 86 – Emissions Standards; CARB Title 13; SAE J1715

description: Result of the end-of-line quality gate inspection for this vehicle build. 'conditional_pass' indicates the vehicle passed with minor deviations requiring dealer rectification. Drives release-to-shipping authorization.

description: Number of rework operations performed on this vehicle build. A count greater than zero indicates quality issues encountered during assembly. Used for PPM (Parts Per Million) defect rate calculation and FMEA analysis.

description: Indicates whether this vehicle build has been flagged for rework due to a detected defect or non-conformance (True = rework required). Distinct from hold_flag; a vehicle can be in rework while not on hold.

description: The planned calendar date on which this vehicle was scheduled to be built per the production plan (MRP/MES schedule). Used to measure schedule adherence and production plan vs. actual variance.

description: Indicates whether this vehicle build is part of the official Start of Production (SOP) launch batch (True = SOP vehicle). SOP vehicles are subject to enhanced quality scrutiny and PPAP sign-off requirements.
reference: APQP – Advanced Product Quality Planning; PPAP – Production Part Approval Process; IATF 16949

description: Actual timestamp when the stamping stage was completed and the body panels were released to the body shop. Null if stage not yet completed.
reference: Apriso/Dassault MES – Stage Tracking; IATF 16949 Clause 8.5.1

description: Actual timestamp when the vehicle body panels entered the stamping stage. Used for stage-level cycle time analysis and production flow monitoring.
reference: Apriso/Dassault MES – Stage Tracking; IATF 16949 Clause 8.5.1

description: The unique serial number of the transmission unit installed in this vehicle. Used for drivetrain genealogy, warranty claims, and component-level recall traceability.
reference: IATF 16949 Clause 8.5.2 – Traceability; NHTSA Recall Regulations 49 CFR Part 573

description: Actual timestamp when the trim assembly stage was completed. Null if stage not yet completed.
reference: Apriso/Dassault MES – Stage Tracking; IATF 16949 Clause 8.5.1

description: Actual timestamp when the vehicle entered the trim assembly stage for interior fitting, electrical harness installation, and dashboard assembly.
reference: Apriso/Dassault MES – Stage Tracking; IATF 16949 Clause 8.5.1

description: Timestamp of the most recent update to this vehicle build record. Used for incremental data pipeline processing, audit trails, and detecting stale records in the Silver layer.
reference: IATF 16949 Clause 7.5 – Documented Information; ISO 9001 Clause 7.5

description: Internal product/model code identifying the vehicle nameplate and variant being built (e.g., F150-XLT-4WD). Maps to the PLM product structure in Siemens Teamcenter. Used for BOM resolution and build sequence planning.

description: The globally unique 17-character Vehicle Identification Number (VIN) assigned to this vehicle unit at body-in-white or VIN stamping stage. Conforms to ISO 3779 / NHTSA VIN standard. SSOT for vehicle-level traceability across manufacturing, sales, and after-sales domains.
reference: ISO 3779; NHTSA 49 CFR Part 565 – VIN Requirements

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each capacity plan record in the manufacturing domain. Primary key for the capacity_plan data product.

Reference to the work center identified as the capacity bottleneck constraining overall line throughput during this planning period. Used to prioritize capacity improvement investments and focus S&OP constraint management.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP — Bottleneck Work Center (CM21); Theory of Constraints; IATF 16949 Clause 6.1

description: Foreign key linking to finance.budget_plan. Business justification: Capacity plans drive headcount and capital budgets; linking capacity_plan to budget_plan enables integrated S&OP-to-financial-plan alignment. Automotive OEMs reconcile capacity plans with financial bu

Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Capacity plans are time-bounded by fiscal periods for quarterly and annual planning cycles. Automotive OEMs align capacity utilization reporting to fiscal periods for integrated financial-operational

Foreign key linking to sales.fleet_contract. Business justification: Fleet contracts with large committed volumes are primary inputs to capacity planning. Automotive capacity planners must know which fleet contracts are driving capacity requirements to justify headcoun

Foreign key linking to vehicle.model. Business justification: Capacity plans are built per vehicle model to determine line investment, headcount, and takt time requirements. S&OP and capital expenditure approval processes require capacity plans linked to specifi
Reference to the manufacturing plant for which this capacity plan is defined. Links to the manufacturing.plant master record.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP — Work Center / Plant; IATF 16949 Clause 7.1.3

description: Reference to the specific production line (e.g., final assembly line, body shop line, paint line) within the plant for which capacity is being planned. Links to the manufacturing.work_center or production line master.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP — Production Line; Apriso/Dassault MES — Line Configuration

description: Reference to the shift pattern (e.g., 1-shift, 2-shift, 3-shift) applied in this capacity plan. Shift pattern determines available production hours and directly impacts rated capacity calculation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP — Shift Definition; IATF 16949 Clause 6.1

description: Foreign key linking to engineering.vehicle_program. Business justification: Capacity planning aligns plant capacity with each vehicle program; capacity_plan needs vehicle_program reference.

Foreign key linking to finance.wbs_element. Business justification: Capacity expansion plans requiring CAPEX investment are linked to WBS elements for investment program controlling. capacity_plan.capex_amount is a plain attribute indicating CAPEX relevance; the WBS F
Date and time when the capacity plan was formally approved by the designated authority. Marks the transition from 'submitted' to 'approved' status in the S&OP governance process.
reference: IATF 16949 Clause 5.3; SAP S/4HANA PP — Approval Workflow

description: Name or employee identifier of the S&OP or manufacturing leadership authority who approved this capacity plan. Required for audit trail and governance compliance under IATF 16949.
reference: IATF 16949 Clause 5.3 — Organizational Roles; SAP S/4HANA PP — Approval Workflow

description: Total scheduled production hours available in the planning period after accounting for planned downtime, shift breaks, and scheduled maintenance. Used in conjunction with rated_capacity_jph to compute rated capacity in units.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP — Available Capacity; IATF 16949 Clause 6.1

description: Narrative description of the primary capacity constraint at the bottleneck work center (e.g., 'Paint oven cycle time limits throughput to 58 JPH', 'Robotic welding cell maintenance window reduces available hours'). Supports root cause analysis and CapEx justification.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP — Capacity Planning Notes; IATF 16949 Clause 6.1

description: Difference between planned_production_units and demonstrated_capacity_units, representing the capacity shortfall or surplus for the planning period. Positive value indicates a capacity gap requiring corrective action (overtime, additional shifts, CapEx); negative value indicates surplus capacity.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP — Capacity Leveling; S&OP Process; IATF 16949 Clause 6.1

description: Classification of the capacity planning methodology used. 'rough_cut' (RCCP — Rough Cut Capacity Planning) for high-level S&OP; 'detailed' for work-center-level CRP (Capacity Requirements Planning); 'constraint_based' for Theory of Constraints analysis; 'what_if' for scenario planning and CapEx evaluation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP — RCCP/CRP; APICS CPIM Body of Knowledge

description: Planned capacity utilization rate expressed as a percentage, calculated as (planned_production_units / rated_capacity_units) × 100. Drives S&OP decisions on overtime, additional shifts, and capital investment. Target thresholds are typically 85–95% for automotive assembly.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP — Capacity Utilization; IATF 16949 Clause 6.1; S&OP Best Practice

description: Estimated capital expenditure amount required to address the capacity gap identified in this plan. Expressed in the plant's operating currency. Used for CapEx budget submissions and investment approval processes.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/CO — Investment Management; IATF 16949 Clause 6.1

description: Indicates whether this capacity plan identifies a requirement for capital investment (CapEx) to close a capacity gap (e.g., new tooling, additional robots, facility expansion). Triggers CapEx approval workflow in SAP FI/CO.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/CO — CapEx Planning; IATF 16949 Clause 6.1

description: Hours allocated for model changeovers, tooling changes, and line reconfigurations during the planning period. Relevant for mixed-model assembly lines producing multiple vehicle variants (e.g., ICE and BEV on the same line).
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP — Setup Time; Apriso/Dassault MES; IATF 16949 Clause 8.5.1

description: Date and time when this capacity plan record was first created in the system. Provides the audit trail creation anchor for data lineage and compliance purposes.
reference: IATF 16949 Clause 7.5 — Documented Information; ISO 8601 Timestamp Standard

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary values in this capacity plan record (e.g., USD, EUR, MXN, CNY). Ensures consistent financial reporting across multi-plant, multi-currency manufacturing operations.
reference: ISO 4217 — Currency Codes; SAP S/4HANA FI — Currency Configuration

description: Planned number of vehicles to be produced per working day during the planning period, derived from planned_production_units / working_days. Used for daily MES scheduling and shift-level production targets.
