description: Actual production output achieved in a comparable prior period, used as the realistic baseline for capacity planning. Reflects real-world constraints such as quality losses, minor stoppages, and changeovers. Key input to APQP (Advanced Product Quality Planning) capacity studies.
reference: IATF 16949 Clause 8.4.1 — PPAP Capacity Study; APQP Reference Manual

description: Planned End of Production (EOP) date for the vehicle model or program covered by this capacity plan. Marks the milestone when production of the model is scheduled to cease. Used for capacity wind-down planning and asset redeployment decisions.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM — Program Milestones; APQP Reference Manual; IATF 16949 Clause 8.1

description: Fiscal year (FY) to which this capacity plan belongs, used for CapEx (Capital Expenditure) alignment, budget planning, and financial reporting. Format: FY followed by 4-digit year (e.g., FY2025).
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/CO — Fiscal Year; IATF 16949 Clause 6.1

description: Total direct labor headcount required to execute the planned production volume during the planning period. Used for workforce planning, hiring decisions, and labor cost budgeting in SuccessFactors and SAP CO.
reference: SuccessFactors — Workforce Planning; SAP S/4HANA CO — Labor Planning; IATF 16949 Clause 7.1.2

description: Indicates whether the capacity plan is constrained by a bottleneck work center that limits overall line throughput below the planned production target. When True, the bottleneck_work_center_id and bottleneck_constraint_description fields are populated.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP — Bottleneck Scheduling; Theory of Constraints; IATF 16949 Clause 6.1

description: Automotive model year (MY) for which this capacity plan is prepared. Capacity plans are typically aligned to MY production programs, SOP (Start of Production), and EOP (End of Production) milestones.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP — Production Program; NHTSA MY Designation

description: Number of production shifts planned per working day for this capacity plan (e.g., 1, 2, or 3 shifts). Directly determines available production hours and total capacity for the planning period.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP — Shift Model; IATF 16949 Clause 6.1

description: Last calendar date of the planning period covered by this capacity plan record. Together with plan_start_date, defines the planning window for capacity supply and demand balancing.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP — Capacity Planning; ISO 8601 Date Standard

description: Externally-known, human-readable business identifier for the capacity plan record, used in S&OP (Sales and Operations Planning) communications, SAP PP capacity planning transactions (CM01/CM21), and cross-functional reporting. Format: CP-{PLANT}-{YEAR}-{MONTH}-{SEQ}.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP — Capacity Planning (CM01); IATF 16949 Clause 6.1

description: First calendar date of the planning period covered by this capacity plan record. Used to align capacity supply with demand signals from S&OP and MRP (Material Requirements Planning) runs.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP — Capacity Planning; ISO 8601 Date Standard

description: Current lifecycle state of the capacity plan record. 'draft' indicates work-in-progress; 'submitted' indicates pending approval; 'approved' indicates sign-off by S&OP leadership; 'active' indicates the operative plan driving production scheduling; 'superseded' indicates replaced by a newer version; 'archived' indicates historical record.
reference: IATF 16949 Clause 6.1; SAP S/4HANA PP — Capacity Planning

description: Version identifier of the capacity plan, enabling management of multiple planning iterations (e.g., v1.0, v2.0) within the same planning period. Supports S&OP cycle versioning and plan-over-plan variance analysis.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP — Capacity Planning; IATF 16949 Clause 6.1

description: Total planned downtime hours for the production line during the planning period, including scheduled maintenance, tooling changeovers, model changeovers, and planned shutdowns. Reduces available production hours from the theoretical maximum.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PM — Maintenance Planning; Apriso/Dassault MES; IATF 16949 Clause 8.5.1

description: Subset of planned downtime hours specifically allocated to preventive and predictive maintenance activities on production equipment. Tracked separately from changeover downtime for maintenance KPI reporting and SAP PM integration.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PM — Preventive Maintenance; IATF 16949 Clause 8.5.1

description: Total overtime hours planned for the production line during the planning period to meet production targets beyond standard shift capacity. Feeds into workforce cost planning and labor budget in SAP CO.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP/CO — Capacity Planning; SuccessFactors Workforce Planning

description: Number of vehicles planned for production in the planning period as agreed in the S&OP process. Represents the demand signal that capacity must satisfy. Compared against rated and demonstrated capacity to identify gaps.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP — Production Program; S&OP Process

description: Granularity of the capacity planning horizon for this record. 'weekly' supports short-term MES scheduling; 'monthly' supports S&OP cycles; 'quarterly' and 'annual' support CapEx and strategic workforce planning.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP — Capacity Planning Horizon; IATF 16949 Clause 6.1

description: Demand scenario underpinning this capacity plan. 'base' reflects the consensus S&OP forecast; 'optimistic' reflects upside demand; 'pessimistic' reflects downside risk; 'stretch' reflects maximum achievable output. Enables scenario comparison for executive decision-making.
reference: S&OP Best Practice; SAP S/4HANA PP — Planning Scenario

description: Powertrain technology type planned for production in this capacity plan. ICE = Internal Combustion Engine; HEV = Hybrid Electric Vehicle; PHEV = Plug-in Hybrid Electric Vehicle; BEV = Battery Electric Vehicle; FCEV = Fuel Cell Electric Vehicle. Impacts tooling, line configuration, and capacity requirements.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP — Production Program; EPA Emissions Standards; CAFE Regulations

description: Theoretical maximum production rate expressed in Jobs Per Hour (JPH) for the production line under ideal conditions. Derived from takt time and line design specifications. Used as the baseline for capacity utilization calculations in S&OP.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP — Capacity Definition; IATF 16949 Clause 6.1; SAE J3061

description: Theoretical maximum number of vehicles that can be produced in the planning period, calculated as rated_capacity_jph × available_hours. Represents the upper bound of production capacity for S&OP and CapEx planning.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP — Capacity Planning (CM01); IATF 16949 Clause 6.1

description: Source system identifier for this capacity plan record in SAP S/4HANA PP (Production Planning) module, used for data lineage traceability and reconciliation between the lakehouse Silver layer and the operational ERP system.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP — Capacity Planning (CM01/CM21); Data Lineage Best Practice

description: Planned Start of Production (SOP) date for the vehicle model or program covered by this capacity plan. Marks the milestone when full-rate production is scheduled to begin. Critical for capacity ramp-up planning and APQP milestone tracking.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM — Program Milestones; APQP Reference Manual; IATF 16949 Clause 8.1

description: Planned takt time in seconds for the production line during this planning period, representing the rate at which vehicles must be produced to meet customer demand. Takt time = available production time / planned production units. Core metric in JIS (Just-in-Sequence) and line balancing.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP — Work Center Takt Time; Apriso/Dassault MES; IATF 16949 Clause 6.1

description: Date and time when this capacity plan record was last modified. Tracks plan revisions through the S&OP cycle and supports change management auditing.
reference: IATF 16949 Clause 7.5 — Documented Information; ISO 8601 Timestamp Standard

description: Number of scheduled working days in the planning period after excluding weekends, public holidays, and planned plant shutdowns. Used to compute daily production targets and available hours.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP — Factory Calendar; IATF 16949 Clause 6.1

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each manufacturing plant or assembly facility. Primary key for the plant master record, referenced across MES, SAP PP, and logistics domains.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module - Plant Master

description: Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Each manufacturing plant operates under a legal entity (company code) for statutory financial reporting, intercompany transactions, and SAP FI/CO integration. Automotive OEMs always map plant-to-compa
Foreign key linking to compliance.jurisdiction. Business justification: Plant regulatory reporting requires knowing the jurisdiction (country/region) governing emissions and safety permits.
Primary street address of the plant facility. Used for logistics, regulatory filings, and official correspondence. Classified as confidential organizational contact data.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module - Plant Address; NHTSA Facility Registration

description: Secondary address line for the plant facility (e.g., building number, gate, or suite). Supplements address_line1 for complete facility location identification.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module - Plant Address

description: Indicates whether the plant is equipped with Automated Guided Vehicle (AGV) systems for material transport on the shop floor. Used for automation capability classification and MES integration configuration.
reference: Apriso/Dassault MES - AGV Integration; SAE International Automation Standards

description: Maximum number of vehicles or assemblies the plant is designed to produce per year under normal two-shift operations. Used for capacity planning, CapEx justification, and production scheduling in SAP PP.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module - Capacity Planning; IATF 16949

description: City or municipality where the plant is physically located. Used for logistics planning, workforce commute analysis, and regulatory jurisdiction determination.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module - Plant Master

description: The date on which the plant was officially commissioned and handed over for production operations. Distinct from SOP date; represents the physical readiness milestone. Used for asset lifecycle management and depreciation calculations in SAP AM.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PM Module - Functional Location; ISO 14001

description: SAP Controlling (CO) cost center code assigned to the plant for financial cost allocation, CapEx tracking, and R&D expenditure reporting. Used in management accounting and EBITDA analysis.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO Module - Cost Center Accounting

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code indicating the country where the plant is physically located (e.g., 'USA', 'DEU', 'CHN'). Used for regulatory compliance, CAFE reporting, and logistics routing.
reference: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3; EPA CAFE Reporting; UNECE Vehicle Regulations

description: Timestamp when the plant master record was first created in the system. Used for data lineage, audit trail, and record lifecycle management in the Databricks Silver Layer.

ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the local currency used in plant-level financial reporting and cost accounting (e.g., 'USD', 'EUR', 'CNY'). Used in SAP FI/CO for cost center reporting and CapEx tracking.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; SAP S/4HANA FI Module

description: Maximum number of vehicles or assemblies the plant can produce per day (Jobs Per Day - JPD). Derived from takt time and shift configuration. Used for daily production scheduling and MES throughput planning.
reference: Apriso/Dassault MES - Capacity Configuration; SAP S/4HANA PP

description: The official End of Production (EOP) date when the plant is scheduled to cease production of the current model or permanently close. Used for capacity planning, workforce transition, and asset disposal planning.
reference: IATF 16949 - APQP; SAP S/4HANA PP Module - Plant Master

description: EPA-assigned facility identifier for emissions and environmental compliance reporting. Required for CAFE (Corporate Average Fuel Economy) reporting and Clean Air Act compliance.
reference: EPA - Environmental Protection Agency; CAFE Standards; CARB Regulations

description: Total manufacturing floor area of the plant in square meters. Used for facility planning, CapEx allocation, insurance valuation, and ISO 14001 environmental footprint reporting.
reference: ISO 14001 Environmental Management; SAP S/4HANA PM Module - Functional Location

description: Indicates whether the plant holds a current IATF 16949 automotive quality management system certification. Mandatory for OEM supplier qualification and customer-specific requirements compliance.
reference: IATF 16949:2016 - International Automotive Task Force Quality Management

description: Indicates whether the plant holds a current ISO 14001 Environmental Management System certification. Required for EPA compliance reporting and sustainability disclosures.
reference: ISO 14001:2015 Environmental Management Systems; EPA Regulations

description: Indicates whether the plant holds a current ISO 9001 Quality Management System certification. Required for supplier qualification and regulatory compliance reporting.
Geographic latitude coordinate of the plant in decimal degrees (WGS 84). Used for geospatial analytics, logistics network optimization, and supply chain mapping.
Geographic longitude coordinate of the plant in decimal degrees (WGS 84). Used for geospatial analytics, logistics network optimization, and supply chain mapping.

Full name of the plant manager or general manager responsible for overall plant operations. Used for escalation routing, regulatory correspondence, and organizational reporting. Not classified as PII as this is a business role reference.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PM Module; SuccessFactors HR

description: The plant identifier as registered in the Apriso/Dassault MES system. Used for cross-system reconciliation between SAP PP production orders and MES shop floor execution records. Enables traceability from ERP to shop floor.
reference: Apriso/Dassault MES - Plant Configuration; IATF 16949 Traceability

description: The current Model Year (MY) of vehicles being produced at the plant (e.g., '2025'). Used for production scheduling, regulatory compliance submissions, and CAFE reporting alignment.
reference: NHTSA Model Year Definition; EPA CAFE Reporting

business_glossary_term: National Highway Traffic Safety Administration (NHTSA) Facility Identifier
description: NHTSA-assigned facility identifier for the plant. Required for vehicle recall management, safety defect reporting, and Federal Motor Vehicle Safety Standards (FMVSS) compliance submissions.
reference: NHTSA - National Highway Traffic Safety Administration; DOT FMVSS

description: Total count of active production or assembly lines within the plant. Used for capacity planning, MES line configuration, and production scheduling.
reference: Apriso/Dassault MES - Line Configuration; SAP S/4HANA PP

description: Number of production shifts operated per day at the plant (typically 1, 2, or 3). Drives capacity calculations, workforce planning in SuccessFactors, and MES scheduling.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module - Shift Planning; SuccessFactors Workforce Planning

description: Current lifecycle state of the plant. 'active' indicates full production; 'idle' indicates temporary suspension; 'ramp_up' indicates pre-SOP commissioning phase; 'ramp_down' indicates post-EOP wind-down; 'shutdown' indicates planned or unplanned stoppage; 'decommissioned' indicates permanently closed.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module - Plant Master; IATF 16949

description: Indicates whether the plant has the infrastructure to perform OTA (Over-the-Air) software updates on vehicles during the final assembly or PDI (Pre-Delivery Inspection) process. Relevant for connected vehicle production.
reference: Geotab/Bosch IoT - OTA Update Infrastructure; UNECE WP.29 Cybersecurity Regulation

description: Primary telephone number for the plant facility. Used for operational communications, emergency contact, and supplier/logistics coordination. Classified as confidential organizational contact data.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module - Plant Master

description: Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the plant within SAP S/4HANA and MES systems (e.g., 'DETF01', 'USNA02'). Used as the business key across ERP, MES, and logistics integrations. Maps to SAP Organizational Unit - Plant Code.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module - Plant Master; IATF 16949

description: Official full name of the manufacturing plant or assembly facility (e.g., 'Detroit Final Assembly Plant No. 1'). Used in reporting, regulatory submissions, and communications.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module - Plant Master

description: Categorical classification of the plant's primary manufacturing function. Values include stamping (metal forming), body_shop (body-in-white welding), paint (paint application), final_assembly (vehicle assembly), ckd (Completely Knocked Down assembly), skd (Semi Knocked Down assembly), powertrain (engine/transmission), logistics (parts distribution). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: stamping|body_shop|paint|final_assembly|ckd|skd|powertrain|logistics — promote to reference product]
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module; IATF 16949 Clause 4.1

description: Postal or ZIP code of the plant location. Used for logistics routing, tax jurisdiction assignment, and regulatory reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module - Plant Address; ISO 3166

description: Classification of powertrain technologies assembled or manufactured at this plant. ICE (Internal Combustion Engine), EV (Electric Vehicle), HEV (Hybrid Electric Vehicle), PHEV (Plug-in Hybrid Electric Vehicle), FCEV (Fuel Cell Electric Vehicle), or mixed. Used for EPA emissions compliance, CAFE reporting, and R&D investment tracking.
reference: EPA CAFE Standards; CARB ZEV Mandate; UNECE Emissions Regulations

description: The primary vehicle segment produced at this plant. Values: car (passenger cars), truck (light/heavy trucks), suv (Sport Utility Vehicles), commercial_vehicle (vans/buses/heavy commercial), ev (Battery Electric Vehicle), hev (Hybrid Electric Vehicle - HEV), phev (Plug-in Hybrid Electric Vehicle - PHEV), mixed (multiple segments). Drives model year planning and regulatory compliance requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: car|truck|suv|commercial_vehicle|ev|hev|phev|mixed — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: NHTSA Vehicle Classification; EPA CAFE Standards; SAE J1100

description: Geographic or operational region in which the plant is located (e.g., 'North America', 'Europe', 'Asia Pacific'). Used for regional production planning, capacity allocation, and management reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module - Plant Master

business_glossary_term: Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition (SCADA) Enabled Flag
description: Indicates whether the plant operates a SCADA (Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition) system for real-time monitoring and control of manufacturing processes, utilities, and equipment.
reference: Apriso/Dassault MES - SCADA Integration; IEC 62443 Industrial Cybersecurity

description: Abbreviated or commonly used name for the plant used in dashboards, shop floor displays, and internal communications (e.g., 'Detroit FA1').
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module - Plant Master

description: The official Start of Production (SOP) date when the plant commenced or is scheduled to commence volume production. Critical milestone for capital planning, workforce ramp-up, and supplier readiness. Aligns with APQP phase-gate milestones.
reference: IATF 16949 - APQP; SAP S/4HANA PP Module - Plant Master

description: State, province, or administrative subdivision where the plant is located (e.g., 'Michigan', 'Bavaria', 'Ontario'). Used for local regulatory compliance, tax jurisdiction, and workforce reporting.
reference: DOT Federal Motor Vehicle Safety Standards; CARB Regulations

description: The rate at which vehicles must be produced to meet customer demand, expressed in seconds per unit. Calculated as available production time divided by customer demand rate. Fundamental to JIS (Just-in-Sequence) scheduling and line balancing in MES.
reference: SAE International - Manufacturing Engineering; Apriso/Dassault MES

description: IANA time zone identifier for the plant location (e.g., 'America/Detroit', 'Europe/Berlin'). Used for MES shift scheduling, production timestamp normalization, and cross-plant reporting in the Databricks lakehouse.

Indicates whether the plant workforce is represented by a labor union. Impacts workforce planning, shift scheduling, overtime policies, and collective bargaining agreement compliance in SuccessFactors.
reference: SuccessFactors Workforce Planning; DOT Labor Regulations

description: Timestamp when the plant master record was last modified. Used for change tracking, data synchronization between SAP S/4HANA and the Databricks lakehouse, and audit compliance.
Total number of employees (direct and indirect labor) currently employed at the plant. Used for workforce planning, capacity analysis, and regulatory headcount reporting in SuccessFactors.
reference: SuccessFactors HR - Workforce Planning; DOT Labor Reporting

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each production line record within the manufacturing data platform. Serves as the primary key for the production_line entity. Role: MASTER_RESOURCE.

Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Line‑level cost allocation report requires linking each production line to its finance cost center; experts expect a cost_center_id instead of the denormalized code.

Reference to the manufacturing plant where this production line is physically located. Links to the plant master record for capacity planning, OEE reporting, and regulatory compliance reporting by facility.
reference: IATF 16949 §4.4 – Quality Management System; SAP PP Plant Master

description: The SAP S/4HANA PP (Production Planning) work center identifier corresponding to this production line. Enables direct linkage between the lakehouse production_line master and SAP production orders, capacity planning, and cost center assignments in SAP CO (Controlling).
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP – Work Center Master Data; IATF 16949 §8.5.1

description: Foreign key linking to engineering.vehicle_program. Business justification: Line scheduling and line‑to‑program assignment use vehicle_program_id to match line capabilities with program requirements.

Foreign key linking to finance.wbs_element. Business justification: Production line installation, retooling, and capacity expansion projects are tracked against WBS elements for CAPEX project controlling. Automotive OEMs create WBS elements for each line retooling or

business_glossary_term: Automated Guided Vehicle (AGV) Integration Enabled Flag
description: Indicates whether this production line is integrated with an AGV (Automated Guided Vehicle) system for automated material delivery, WIP (Work in Progress) transport, or carrier movement. True = AGV-integrated; False = manual or conventional conveyor-based material handling. Used for automation investment tracking and MES configuration.
reference: IATF 16949 §8.5.1 – Control of Production; ISO 3691-4 – Industrial Trucks Safety

description: Classification of the overall automation level of the production line: manual (predominantly human-operated), semi_automated (mix of manual and robotic operations), highly_automated (majority robotic with human oversight), or fully_automated (lights-out or near-lights-out operation). Used for workforce planning, CapEx investment analysis, and Industry 4.0 maturity assessments.
reference: IATF 16949 §8.5.1; SAE J3016 – Automation Levels (adapted for manufacturing); VDI 2860

description: Indicates the assembly kit type this line is configured to process: CKD (Completely Knocked Down — full kit assembly from parts), SKD (Semi Knocked Down — partial pre-assembly), CBU (Completely Built Up — no kit assembly, line-off only), or multi (capable of multiple kit types). Relevant for export assembly plants and regulatory homologation.
reference: UNECE WP.29 – Vehicle Regulations; IATF 16949 §8.5.1; Customs Tariff Classification

description: Type of conveyor or carrier system used to move vehicles or assemblies along this production line: overhead_conveyor (hanging carrier), floor_conveyor (ground-level chain/belt), skillet (moving platform for final assembly), agv_carrier (autonomous guided vehicle carrier), stationary (fixed-position assembly), or mixed (combination of types).
reference: IATF 16949 §8.5.1 – Control of Production; ISO 3691-4 – Industrial Trucks

description: Timestamp when this production line master record was first created in the data platform (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Provides audit trail for data governance, lineage tracking, and IATF 16949 record retention compliance. Maps to RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED category for MASTER_RESOURCE role.
reference: IATF 16949 §7.5.3 – Control of Documented Information; ISO 9001 §7.5.3

business_glossary_term: Current Actual Jobs Per Hour (JPH) Rate
description: The current actual or most recently measured throughput rate of the production line in Jobs Per Hour (JPH), reflecting real-world performance after accounting for constraints, model mix, and current configuration. Used to compare against designed_jph for capacity gap analysis and production scheduling.
reference: IATF 16949 §9.1.1 – Monitoring and Measurement; Apriso MES Production Rate

business_glossary_term: Designed Jobs Per Hour (JPH) Throughput Rate
description: The engineered design throughput capacity of the production line expressed in Jobs Per Hour (JPH) — the number of vehicle units or assemblies the line is designed to produce per hour under optimal conditions. This is the nameplate capacity used for capacity planning, takt time calculation, and OEE baseline. Maps to MEASUREMENT_OR_VALUE category for MASTER_RESOURCE role.
reference: SAE J1739 – FMEA; IATF 16949 §8.5.1.1 – Control Plan; SAP PP Capacity Definition

description: Average energy consumption of this production line in kilowatt-hours (kWh) per vehicle unit produced. Used for ISO 14001 environmental management reporting, carbon footprint calculation, energy efficiency benchmarking, and EPA/CARB manufacturing emissions compliance.
reference: ISO 14001 §9.1 – Environmental Performance Evaluation; ISO 50001 – Energy Management; EPA 40 CFR Part 98

description: The planned or actual End of Production (EOP) date for this production line — the date on which series production of the current vehicle program is scheduled to cease. Used for capacity reallocation planning, tooling disposition, and supply chain wind-down management. Nullable for lines with no defined EOP.
reference: IATF 16949 §8.3.6 – Design and Development Changes; SAP PP Production Program

description: Total floor space occupied by this production line in square meters, including the line footprint, buffer zones, and material staging areas. Used for facility capacity planning, real estate cost allocation, and plant layout optimization.
reference: ISO 14001 §8.1 – Operational Planning; OSHA 29 CFR 1910.22 – Walking-Working Surfaces

description: Current IATF 16949 (International Automotive Task Force) quality management system audit status for this production line: compliant (no non-conformances), minor_nc (minor non-conformance identified), major_nc (major non-conformance — corrective action required), suspended (production suspended pending corrective action), or not_audited (not yet subject to IATF audit).
reference: IATF 16949:2016 – Quality Management System Requirements for Automotive Production

description: Indicates whether this production line operates under JIS (Just-in-Sequence) material delivery sequencing, where parts are delivered to the line in the exact sequence of vehicle build order. True = JIS-enabled; False = standard JIT or batch delivery. Critical for supply chain integration and sequencing system configuration.
reference: IATF 16949 §8.4.3 – Supplier Communication; SAE J4000 – Lean Manufacturing

description: Date of the most recent IATF 16949 quality management system audit conducted on this production line. Used for audit cycle management, corrective action tracking, and regulatory compliance reporting to OEM customers and certification bodies.

Date of the most recent planned preventive maintenance activity performed on this production line. Used for maintenance scheduling in SAP PM (Plant Maintenance), OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness) calculation, and IATF 16949 preventive maintenance compliance.
reference: IATF 16949 §8.5.1.5 – Total Productive Maintenance; SAP PM Maintenance Order

description: Externally-known alphanumeric code that uniquely identifies the production line within the plant and across the enterprise (e.g., FA-01, BS-03, PT-02). Used as the business key in MES, SAP PP, and Apriso/Dassault shop-floor execution systems. Maps to BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER category for MASTER_RESOURCE role.
reference: IATF 16949 §8.5.2 – Identification and Traceability; Apriso/Dassault MES Line Master

description: Physical length of the production line in meters, measured from the first to the last station. Used for facility layout planning, AGV (Automated Guided Vehicle) path configuration, and ergonomic compliance assessments per plant engineering standards.
reference: ISO 14001 §8.1 – Operational Planning; OSHA 29 CFR 1910 – General Industry Standards

description: Human-readable descriptive name of the production line (e.g., 'Final Assembly Line 1 – Truck Platform', 'Body Shop Framing Line – SUV'). Used in reporting dashboards, capacity planning, and operator interfaces. Maps to IDENTITY_LABEL category for MASTER_RESOURCE role.
reference: IATF 16949 §8.5 – Production and Service Provision; SAP PP Work Center Description

description: Current operational lifecycle status of the production line: active (running production), idle (scheduled downtime or no orders), maintenance (planned or unplanned maintenance), ramp_up (pre-SOP commissioning), ramp_down (approaching EOP), or decommissioned (permanently retired). Maps to LIFECYCLE_STATUS category for MASTER_RESOURCE role.
reference: IATF 16949 §8.5.1; SAP PM Equipment Status; Apriso MES Line Status

description: Categorical classification of the production line by manufacturing process area: stamping (metal forming), body_shop (welding/framing), paint (surface treatment), final_assembly (vehicle build), powertrain (engine/transmission), chassis (underbody), or trim (interior/exterior finishing). Maps to CLASSIFICATION_OR_TYPE category for MASTER_RESOURCE role. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: stamping|body_shop|paint|final_assembly|powertrain|chassis|trim|subassembly|battery_pack|quality_gate — promote to reference product]
reference: IATF 16949 §8.5.1 – Control of Production; SAE J4000 – Lean Manufacturing

description: Maximum gross vehicle weight in kilograms that this production line's conveyor, tooling, and fixtures are rated to handle. Constrains which vehicle programs can be assigned to this line and is critical for structural safety compliance and tooling certification.
reference: ISO 3691-4 – Industrial Trucks; OSHA 29 CFR 1910.179 – Overhead Cranes; IATF 16949 §8.5.1

description: The line identifier as registered in the Apriso/Dassault MES (Manufacturing Execution System) for shop floor execution, work order dispatch, and build traceability. Used for cross-system reconciliation between the lakehouse silver layer and the operational MES source system.
reference: ISA-95 – Enterprise-Control System Integration; Apriso/Dassault MES Line Master

description: Indicates whether this production line is capable of producing multiple vehicle models or variants in a mixed sequence on the same line (mixed-model assembly). True = mixed-model capable; False = dedicated single-model line. Drives production scheduling complexity and sequencing logic in MES.
reference: IATF 16949 §8.5.1.1 – Control Plan; SAE J4000 – Lean Manufacturing

description: The model year (MY) or range of model years that this production line is currently tooled and certified to produce (e.g., '2024' or '2024-2026'). Drives SOP (Start of Production) and EOP (End of Production) planning, tooling investment decisions, and homologation scheduling.
reference: IATF 16949 §8.3.2 – Design and Development Planning; NHTSA FMVSS Compliance

description: Date of the next planned preventive maintenance activity scheduled for this production line. Drives maintenance work order creation in SAP PM, production scheduling adjustments, and downtime planning to minimize impact on JPH targets.
reference: IATF 16949 §8.5.1.5 – Total Productive Maintenance; SAP PM Maintenance Planning

description: Total count of individual work stations or assembly stations on this production line. Used for line balancing analysis, ergonomic assessments, and determining the number of operators required per shift. Key input for APQP (Advanced Product Quality Planning) and control plan development.
reference: IATF 16949 §8.3.4.4 – APQP; SAP PP Work Center Station Count

description: Type of paint application system used on this line (applicable to paint shop lines): waterborne (water-based paint — lower VOC emissions), solventborne (solvent-based paint), powder_coat (electrostatic powder coating), or none (non-paint line). Drives EPA/CARB VOC (Volatile Organic Compound) emissions compliance reporting and environmental permit management.
reference: EPA 40 CFR Part 63 Subpart IIII – Paint Stripping and Miscellaneous Surface Coating; CARB Title 17; ISO 14001

description: Identifier or name of the PLC (Programmable Logic Controller) system or network controlling this production line's automated equipment (e.g., 'Siemens S7-1500 Network A', 'Allen-Bradley ControlLogix Zone 3'). Used for maintenance management in SAP PM, SCADA integration, and cybersecurity asset inventory.
reference: IEC 61131-3 – PLC Programming Standards; IATF 16949 §8.5.1; NIST SP 800-82 – ICS Security

description: The powertrain technology types this production line is capable of assembling: ICE (Internal Combustion Engine), HEV (Hybrid Electric Vehicle), PHEV (Plug-in Hybrid Electric Vehicle), BEV (Battery Electric Vehicle), FCEV (Fuel Cell Electric Vehicle), or multi (flexible line supporting multiple powertrain types). Critical for EV transition capacity planning and regulatory compliance with EPA/CARB emissions standards.
reference: EPA 40 CFR Part 86 – Emissions Standards; CARB Title 13; CAFE Standards

description: Number of quality inspection gates or checkpoints integrated into this production line (e.g., in-process inspection stations, end-of-line audit points). Used for quality planning per APQP (Advanced Product Quality Planning), control plan documentation, and IATF 16949 compliance audits.
reference: IATF 16949 §8.6 – Release of Products; APQP Phase 4 – Product and Process Validation

description: Total number of industrial robots (welding, painting, assembly, material handling) deployed on this production line. Used for maintenance planning in SAP PM, automation investment tracking, and Industry 4.0 KPI reporting.
reference: ISO 10218 – Robots and Robotic Devices Safety; IATF 16949 §8.5.1

description: Expiry date of the production line's current safety certification. Used to trigger renewal workflows, prevent operation of uncertified lines, and support regulatory audit readiness for OSHA, DOT, and IATF 16949 compliance.

Current status of the production line's safety certification and compliance with applicable occupational safety standards (e.g., OSHA, CE Machinery Directive, ISO 13849): certified (valid and current), expired (certification lapsed), pending_renewal (renewal in progress), or not_required (exempt from certification requirement).
reference: OSHA 29 CFR 1910 – General Industry; ISO 13849 – Safety of Machinery; EU Machinery Directive 2006/42/EC

business_glossary_term: Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition (SCADA) System Reference
description: Identifier or name of the SCADA (Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition) system monitoring and controlling this production line (e.g., 'Ignition SCADA Plant2-Zone1', 'Wonderware InTouch Line FA-01'). Used for real-time production monitoring, alarm management, and integration with MES and ERP systems.
reference: IEC 62443 – Industrial Cybersecurity; IATF 16949 §8.5.1; NIST SP 800-82

description: Current shift schedule configuration for the production line, indicating the number of operating shifts per day: 1_shift (single day shift), 2_shift (day and afternoon), 3_shift (continuous 24-hour), or 4_shift_rotating (rotating continental schedule). Drives capacity planning, workforce scheduling in SuccessFactors, and MRP calculations in SAP PP.
reference: IATF 16949 §8.5.1; SAP PP Shift Definition; SuccessFactors Workforce Planning

description: The official Start of Production (SOP) date for this production line — the date on which the line commenced or is scheduled to commence series production of the current vehicle program. Used for program milestone tracking, APQP phase gate management, and regulatory homologation scheduling.
reference: IATF 16949 §8.3.2 – Design and Development Planning; APQP Phase 5 – Launch

description: Identifies the operational system of record from which this production line master record was sourced: SAP_PP (SAP S/4HANA Production Planning), Apriso_MES (Apriso/Dassault Manufacturing Execution System), Teamcenter (Siemens Teamcenter PLM), Manual (manually entered), or SCADA (derived from SCADA system configuration). Used for data lineage and reconciliation.
reference: ISA-95 – Enterprise-Control System Integration; IATF 16949 §7.5 – Documented Information

description: The calculated takt time for this production line in seconds — the maximum allowable time to produce one unit to meet customer demand. Derived from available production time divided by customer demand rate. Stored as a master configuration value for scheduling and line balancing in JIS (Just-in-Sequence) operations.
reference: SAE J4000 – Lean Manufacturing; IATF 16949 §8.5.1.1 – Control Plan

description: Timestamp of the most recent update to this production line master record in the data platform (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for change tracking, data freshness monitoring, and incremental ETL processing in the Databricks Lakehouse silver layer.
reference: IATF 16949 §7.5.3 – Control of Documented Information; ISO 9001 §7.5.3

description: The vehicle platform or architecture code that this production line is primarily configured to build (e.g., 'F-150 Gen14', 'GMT800', 'MEB'). A single line may support multiple model variants on the same platform. Used for PLM integration with Siemens Teamcenter and production scheduling.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM – Platform Configuration; IATF 16949 §8.3 – Design and Development

description: Total number of resistance spot weld guns (robotic and manual) deployed on this production line. Applicable primarily to body shop lines. Used for weld quality management per IATF 16949, preventive maintenance scheduling in SAP PM, and SPC (Statistical Process Control) monitoring of weld quality.
reference: IATF 16949 §8.5.1.2 – Control of Special Processes; AWS D8.1M – Automotive Spot Welding

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the master production schedule (MPS) record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Primary key for this entity.

Foreign key linking to finance.budget_plan. Business justification: Production volume schedules are the operational input to financial budget plans (revenue, COGS, material spend). Automotive S&OP process explicitly links production schedule to financial budget; this

Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Production schedules are aligned to fiscal periods for revenue recognition planning and financial forecasting. production_schedule.fiscal_period is a denormalized plain attribute; a proper FK enables

business_glossary_term: Vehicle Model Year Program Id (Foreign Key)
description: Foreign key linking to vehicle.vehicle_model_year_program. Business justification: Annual production schedules are structured around model year programs — freeze horizons, SOP/EOP dates, and regulatory market allocations are all model-year-program-driven. This FK enables production

Foreign key linking to sales.msrp_schedule. Business justification: Production planning teams validate scheduled variants against active MSRP schedules before freezing production plans — ensuring only market-priced configurations are scheduled. This supports the S&OP
Reference to the manufacturing plant where the scheduled production will occur. Corresponds to the SAP Plant organizational unit.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Org Structure; IATF 16949

description: Foreign key linking to vehicle.powertrain_variant. Business justification: Production schedules are planned at powertrain variant level for supplier capacity reservation, battery/engine procurement, and regulatory market allocation. production_schedule has powertrain_type as
Reference to the specific production line (e.g., final assembly, body shop, paint shop, stamping) within the plant where the scheduled build will take place.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Work Center; Apriso/Dassault MES

description: Foreign key linking to manufacturing.production_variant. Business justification: A production schedule entry defines planned volumes for a specific production variant (model/option combination). Adding production_variant_id FK enables variant-level schedule planning, capacity allo

Foreign key linking to supply.scheduling_agreement. Business justification: Production schedules release call-offs against scheduling agreements. The scheduling agreement defines contractual delivery rhythm and volumes; the production schedule triggers actual releases. S&OP a

Foreign key linking to inventory.sku_master. Business justification: Production scheduling in automotive MRP requires linking each schedule to the finished-goods SKU to trigger material requirements planning, safety stock checks, and supplier calloffs. A production pla
Reference to the vehicle model (e.g., sedan, SUV, truck, EV) being scheduled for production. Links to the vehicle master product record.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Material Master; Siemens Teamcenter PLM

description: Foreign key linking to engineering.vehicle_program. Business justification: Production schedules are generated per vehicle program; schedule analytics filter by vehicle_program_id.
Actual number of vehicles produced and confirmed as good units by the MES (Manufacturing Execution System) during the schedule period. Populated upon schedule completion from Apriso/Dassault MES.
reference: Apriso/Dassault MES; SAP S/4HANA PP Goods Receipt; IATF 16949 Clause 9.1

description: Name or employee identifier of the production planning manager who formally approved this schedule version for release to the shop floor. Required for IATF 16949 document control and audit trail.
reference: IATF 16949 Clause 7.5; SAP S/4HANA PP Workflow

description: Timestamp when the production schedule was formally approved for release. Marks the transition from 'draft' to 'released' status and triggers supplier call-off generation.
reference: IATF 16949 Clause 7.5; SAP S/4HANA PP Workflow

description: Manufacturing process stage covered by this schedule record. Reflects the major shop areas in automotive assembly: stamping (metal forming), body shop (welding/joining), paint (surface treatment), final assembly (powertrain/trim installation), or trim-chassis-final (TCF integrated line).
reference: Apriso/Dassault MES; SAP S/4HANA PP Routing; IATF 16949 Clause 8.5

description: Total available production capacity in hours for this schedule period on the assigned production line, accounting for planned downtime, maintenance windows, and shift patterns.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Capacity Planning; IATF 16949 Clause 8.1

description: Body style classification of the vehicles scheduled for production. Used for production line configuration, tooling setup, and capacity planning by body type.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Material Classification; Siemens Teamcenter PLM

description: Ending sequence number for the JIS (Just-in-Sequence) build order within this schedule period. Together with build_sequence_start defines the range of vehicle sequence numbers assigned to this schedule.
reference: Apriso/Dassault MES; JIS Standard; SAP S/4HANA PP Sequencing

description: Starting sequence number for the JIS (Just-in-Sequence) build order within this schedule period. Used by MES and AGV systems to sequence vehicle bodies through the production line in the correct order.
reference: Apriso/Dassault MES; JIS Standard; SAP S/4HANA PP Sequencing

description: Classification of the production build type. 'standard' = full series production; 'ckd' = Completely Knocked Down (CKD) kit assembly; 'skd' = Semi Knocked Down (SKD) assembly; 'pilot' = pre-production pilot build; 'pre_series' = pre-series validation build.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP; Siemens Teamcenter PLM; APQP Phase 4-5; IATF 16949

description: Timestamp when supplier delivery call-offs were transmitted to the supply base for this production schedule. Used for OTD (On-Time Delivery) tracking and supplier performance measurement.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Scheduling Agreement; IATF 16949 Clause 8.4; JIT Standard

description: Planned capacity utilization percentage for this schedule period, calculated as (confirmed_quantity × takt_time) / available_capacity_hours × 100. Stored as a planning input from SAP PP capacity leveling, not a derived KPI.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Capacity Leveling; IATF 16949 Clause 8.1

description: Number of vehicles confirmed for production after capacity leveling, constraint resolution, and production planning approval. May differ from planned_quantity due to capacity constraints or material availability.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Capacity Planning; IATF 16949 Clause 8.1

description: Timestamp when this production schedule record was first created in the system, either by an MRP run or manual entry. Audit trail field for data lineage and compliance.
Official End of Production (EOP) date for the vehicle model/model year. Schedules beyond this date are invalid. Used for phase-out planning, last-time-buy procurement, and regulatory deregistration.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP; Siemens Teamcenter PLM; NHTSA Homologation

description: Fiscal year (FY) to which this production schedule belongs. Used for financial planning, CapEx allocation, and EBITDA reporting alignment between production volumes and financial periods.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-CO Fiscal Year; GAAP/IFRS Financial Reporting

description: Date up to which the production schedule is frozen and cannot be changed without formal change control. Protects supplier call-offs and JIT/JIS delivery commitments within the frozen window.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Planning Time Fence; IATF 16949 Clause 8.4; JIT/JIS Standard

description: Boolean flag indicating whether this schedule record is currently within the freeze horizon and locked against changes. True = frozen (no modifications allowed without change control); False = open for revision.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Planning Time Fence; IATF 16949 Clause 8.1

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to this production schedule record. Updated on every revision, status change, or quantity adjustment. Essential for incremental data loading in the Databricks Silver Layer.

The automotive model year (MY) designation for the vehicles being scheduled. Used for regulatory compliance (CAFE, EPA), BOM versioning, and sales planning. Typically one year ahead of the calendar year for SOP planning.
reference: NHTSA FMVSS; EPA CAFE Standards; SAE International MY Definition

description: Total number of vehicles planned for production in this schedule period as determined by the MRP run. Represents the gross requirement before capacity and constraint adjustments.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP-MRP Planned Order Quantity; IATF 16949 Clause 8.1

description: Powertrain technology classification for the vehicles in this schedule. ICE = Internal Combustion Engine; HEV = Hybrid Electric Vehicle; PHEV = Plug-in Hybrid Electric Vehicle; EV = Battery Electric Vehicle; FCEV = Fuel Cell Electric Vehicle. Drives regulatory reporting (CAFE, EPA, CARB) and BOM variant selection.
reference: EPA CAFE Standards; CARB ZEV Mandate; NHTSA FMVSS; SAE J1711

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the primary regulatory market for which these vehicles are being produced. Determines applicable homologation standards (NHTSA/FMVSS for USA, UNECE for Europe, etc.), emissions certification (EPA, CARB, Euro 6), and CAFE compliance tracking. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: USA|CAN|MEX|DEU|GBR|CHN|JPN|KOR|AUS|BRA — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: NHTSA FMVSS; EPA Emissions Standards; UNECE Vehicle Regulations; CARB; ISO 3166-1

description: Business reason code or description for the most recent schedule revision. Examples: material shortage, capacity constraint, demand change, quality hold, tooling failure, model mix adjustment. Required for change control audit trail per IATF 16949.
reference: IATF 16949 Clause 7.5 (Document Control); SAP S/4HANA PP Change Management

description: The principal planned production date (or start date of the weekly/monthly bucket) for this schedule record. Represents the real-world business event date when production is planned to occur.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Planned Order; IATF 16949

description: End date of the production schedule period. For daily schedules this equals schedule_date; for weekly/monthly buckets this marks the last day of the planning period.

Free-text field for production planners to record contextual notes, exceptions, or special instructions associated with this schedule record. Examples: tooling changeover notes, quality hold references, special customer order constraints.

Externally-known alphanumeric identifier for the production schedule record, sourced from SAP PP (Production Planning). Used for cross-system reference and communication with supply chain and manufacturing teams.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module; IATF 16949 Clause 8.1

description: Current lifecycle state of the production schedule record. 'draft' = initial planning; 'released' = approved for execution; 'frozen' = within freeze horizon, no changes allowed; 'in_execution' = active shop floor production; 'completed' = all planned units built; 'cancelled' = schedule voided.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP; IATF 16949 Clause 8.1; Apriso/Dassault MES

description: Time granularity of the production schedule record. 'daily' = day-level MPS; 'weekly' = weekly bucket plan; 'monthly' = monthly capacity plan. Determines the planning horizon and supplier call-off frequency.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP-MRP Planning Horizon; IATF 16949 Clause 8.1

description: Incremental version number of the production schedule. Each MRP (Material Requirements Planning) run or manual revision increments this counter, enabling version comparison and rollback analysis.
Precise timestamp when production is scheduled to complete for this schedule record. Used for shift planning, takt time validation, and downstream logistics coordination.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Work Order Scheduling; Apriso/Dassault MES

description: Precise timestamp when production is scheduled to begin on the line for this schedule record. Used for JIS (Just-in-Sequence) sequencing, AGV routing, and supplier call-off timing.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Work Order Scheduling; Apriso/Dassault MES; JIS Standard

description: Shift configuration planned for this production schedule period. Determines available production hours and workforce requirements. Single = 1 shift/day; Double = 2 shifts/day; Triple = 3 shifts/day; Weekend = weekend-only production.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Capacity Planning; SuccessFactors Workforce Planning

description: Official Start of Production (SOP) date for the vehicle model/model year being scheduled. Marks the formal launch of series production. Used to validate that schedule dates fall within the active production window.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP; Siemens Teamcenter PLM Program Management; APQP Phase 5

description: Boolean flag indicating whether supplier delivery call-offs (JIT/JIS delivery schedules) have been generated and transmitted to suppliers based on this production schedule. True = call-offs sent; False = pending call-off generation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Scheduling Agreement; JIT/JIS Standard; IATF 16949 Clause 8.4

description: Planned takt time in seconds per vehicle unit for this production schedule. Takt time = available production time divided by customer demand rate. Drives line balancing, AGV pacing, and JIS sequencing intervals.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Rate Routing; Apriso/Dassault MES; SAE Manufacturing Standards

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each shift definition record in the manufacturing plant reference master.
Reference to the manufacturing plant at which this shift definition applies. Used to scope shift schedules to specific production facilities.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP – Work Center / Plant Assignment

description: Reference to the specific production line (e.g., stamping, body shop, paint, final assembly) to which this shift definition is assigned. Enables line-level OEE and scheduling.
reference: Apriso/Dassault MES – Shop Floor Line Configuration; SAP S/4HANA PP – Work Center

description: Indicates whether Automated Guided Vehicle (AGV) operations are active during this shift. Used for shop floor automation scheduling and safety protocol management in MES and SCADA integrations.
reference: Apriso/Dassault MES – AGV Integration; SAE International – Automated Manufacturing Safety

description: Total scheduled break time in minutes for the shift (e.g., 30 minutes for a single meal break, 50 minutes for two breaks). Used to reconcile gross shift hours against planned production hours.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP – Break Schedule; DOT – Hours of Service Regulations

description: Textual description of the break schedule for the shift, including break times and durations (e.g., '10:00–10:15 AM rest break; 12:30–13:00 PM meal break'). Supports workforce planning and ergonomic compliance documentation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP – Break Schedule; DOT – Occupational Health and Safety

description: Timestamp when this shift definition record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and IATF 16949 document control compliance.
reference: IATF 16949 – Document Control; ISO 9001 – Quality Records

description: Seven-character binary string (Mon–Sun) indicating which days of the week this shift runs (e.g., '1111100' = Monday to Friday, '0000011' = Saturday and Sunday). Used by MES and SAP PP for automated shift calendar generation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP – Factory Calendar; Apriso/Dassault MES – Shift Calendar

description: Calendar date from which this shift definition becomes valid and available for production scheduling. Supports versioned shift master management and seasonal schedule changes.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP – Shift Validity Period; IATF 16949 – Document Control

description: Planned energy consumption target in kilowatt-hours for the shift. Used for energy efficiency monitoring, ISO 14001 environmental reporting, and CAFE/EPA compliance cost tracking.
reference: ISO 14001 – Environmental Management; EPA – Energy Efficiency Standards; CARB

description: End of Production (EOP) date associated with this shift definition, indicating when the shift schedule is retired for a vehicle programme. Null if no planned end. Used for production wind-down planning.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP – EOP Planning; Siemens Teamcenter PLM – Programme Milestones

description: Calendar date after which this shift definition is no longer valid for production scheduling. Null indicates an open-ended shift definition with no planned end. Supports model year changeovers and SOP/EOP transitions.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP – Shift Validity Period; IATF 16949 – Document Control

description: Total clock hours from shift start to shift end including all breaks and non-production time (e.g., 8.0 hours). Used to distinguish gross capacity from net available production time.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP – Shift Duration; ISO 9001 – Production Capacity Records

description: Planned duration in minutes allocated for shift handover activities between outgoing and incoming crews (e.g., 15 minutes). Included in shift scheduling to ensure continuity of production and quality checks.
reference: Apriso/Dassault MES – Shift Handover; IATF 16949 – Production Continuity

description: Indicates whether this shift record is a reusable template definition (True) rather than a plant/line-specific instantiation (False). Templates are used to standardise shift configurations across multiple plants and lines.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP – Shift Template; Apriso/Dassault MES – Shift Master

description: Native shift identifier in the Apriso/Dassault MES system used for shop floor execution and traceability. Enables cross-system reconciliation between the lakehouse silver layer and the MES source of record.
reference: Apriso/Dassault MES – Shift Master; IATF 16949 – Traceability Requirements

description: Automotive model year (MY) for which this shift definition is applicable (e.g., 2025). Aligns shift scheduling with SOP (Start of Production) and EOP (End of Production) milestones for specific vehicle programmes.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP – Model Year Planning; NHTSA – Vehicle Model Year Definitions

description: Count of distinct scheduled break periods within the shift (e.g., 2 for a morning rest break and a meal break). Used in ergonomic compliance reporting and workforce scheduling.
reference: DOT – Occupational Health and Safety; IATF 16949 – Workforce Management

description: Target OEE percentage for this shift, representing the combined availability, performance, and quality targets (e.g., 85.00%). Used as the benchmark for shift-level OEE reporting and continuous improvement initiatives.
reference: IATF 16949 – OEE Measurement; SAE International – Manufacturing Efficiency Standards

description: Indicates whether this shift definition is classified as an overtime shift, triggering premium pay calculations and overtime reporting in SAP HR and SuccessFactors payroll.
reference: SuccessFactors – Time and Attendance; SAP S/4HANA HCM – Payroll

description: Code referencing the rotating shift pattern or schedule template to which this shift belongs (e.g., 3SHIFT_ROTATING, 2SHIFT_FIXED, CONTINENTAL). Used for workforce rostering and long-term capacity planning.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP – Shift Pattern; SuccessFactors – Work Schedule

description: Planned clock time at which the shift ends production activities. Combined with planned_start_time to derive gross shift duration for capacity and OEE calculations.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP – Shift Time Profile; Apriso/Dassault MES – Shift Schedule

description: Net planned production hours for the shift after deducting all scheduled breaks and non-production time (e.g., 7.5 hours for an 8-hour shift with 30-minute break). Core input for OEE denominator and capacity planning in SAP PP.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP – Available Capacity; IATF 16949 – OEE Measurement

description: Planned clock time at which the shift begins production activities (e.g., 2024-01-15T06:00:00.000+00:00). Used for production order scheduling, JIS sequencing, and workforce rostering.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP – Shift Time Profile; Apriso/Dassault MES – Shift Schedule

description: Pay rate multiplier applied to base wages for this shift (e.g., 1.5000 for time-and-a-half overtime, 1.2500 for night shift differential). Used in payroll cost calculations and labour cost reporting. Classified confidential as it contains compensation data.
reference: SuccessFactors – Payroll; SAP S/4HANA HCM – Wage Types; DOT – Fair Labour Standards

description: Shift operating model classification indicating the overall production schedule structure at the plant (e.g., single_shift, two_shift, three_shift, four_shift_continental). Drives capacity planning and workforce rostering strategy.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP – Capacity Planning; SAE International – Manufacturing Operations

description: Indicates whether a mandatory quality inspection (e.g., PDI, SPC check, FMEA-driven inspection point) is required at the start or end of this shift. Drives SAP QM inspection lot creation and MES quality gate activation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM – Inspection Planning; IATF 16949 – Quality Management; APQP

description: Shift identifier as defined in SAP S/4HANA PP module (Schicht). Used for production order scheduling, capacity planning, and cross-system reconciliation with the ERP source of record.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP – Shift Definition (Schicht); IATF 16949 – Production Planning

business_glossary_term: Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition (SCADA) Zone Code
description: Code identifying the SCADA monitoring zone associated with this shift definition. Used to correlate shift-level production data with real-time SCADA telemetry for process control and quality monitoring.
reference: Apriso/Dassault MES – SCADA Integration; IATF 16949 – Process Monitoring

description: Ordinal position of the shift within a 24-hour production cycle at the plant/line (e.g., 1 = first shift, 2 = second shift, 3 = third shift). Used for JIS sequencing and handover management.
reference: Apriso/Dassault MES – Shift Sequencing; SAP S/4HANA PP – Shift Order

description: Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the shift definition within the plant (e.g., DAY1, AFT2, NGT3, WKD1). Used as the business key in SAP PP and MES integrations.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP – Shift Definition; Apriso/Dassault MES – Shift Master

description: Free-text description providing additional context about the shift definition, including special operating conditions, seasonal variations, or programme-specific notes (e.g., 'Reduced capacity shift during model year changeover').
reference: IATF 16949 – Document Control; SAP S/4HANA PP – Shift Master

description: Human-readable name of the shift (e.g., Day Shift, Afternoon Shift, Night Shift, Weekend Shift). Used in production scheduling dashboards, workforce planning reports, and OEE calculations.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP – Shift Definition; SuccessFactors – Workforce Planning

description: Current lifecycle status of the shift definition. Active shifts are available for production order scheduling; inactive or suspended shifts are excluded from planning runs.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP – Shift Validity; IATF 16949 – Production Planning Records

description: Classification of the shift by its operational period. Drives workforce planning, premium pay calculations, and production capacity modelling. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: day|afternoon|night|weekend|overtime|shutdown — promote to reference product if additional types are required]
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP – Shift Category; SuccessFactors – Time Management

description: Start of Production (SOP) date associated with this shift definition, indicating when the shift schedule becomes operational for a new vehicle programme or model year. Used for production ramp-up planning.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP – SOP Planning; Siemens Teamcenter PLM – Programme Milestones

description: Code identifying the required supervisor role classification for this shift (e.g., PROD_SUPV, AREA_LEAD). Used for workforce planning and shift handover accountability. References the HR role catalogue in SuccessFactors.
reference: SuccessFactors – Job Role Catalogue; SAP S/4HANA HCM – Organisational Management

description: Planned takt time in seconds per unit for this shift, calculated as net available production time divided by customer demand rate. Fundamental metric for line balancing and JIS sequencing in automotive assembly.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP – Takt Time; SAE International – Production Rate Standards

description: Planned number of vehicle units (or assemblies) to be produced during this shift. Used as the production target for OEE performance ratio calculation and daily production reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP – Production Order Quantity; IATF 16949 – OEE Measurement

description: IANA timezone identifier for the plant location where this shift operates (e.g., America/Detroit, Europe/Berlin). Ensures accurate timestamp interpretation for global multi-plant production scheduling and reporting.
reference: ISO 8601 – Date and Time Standards; IANA Timezone Database

description: Timestamp when this shift definition record was last modified. Used for change tracking, data lineage, and incremental ETL processing in the Databricks Lakehouse silver layer.
reference: IATF 16949 – Document Control; ISO 9001 – Quality Records

description: Planned number of direct production workers required for this shift. Used for workforce planning, labour cost budgeting, and integration with SuccessFactors workforce scheduling.
reference: SuccessFactors – Workforce Planning; SAP S/4HANA CO – Labour Cost Planning

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the work center (station) record within the manufacturing data platform. Primary key for the work_center master entity.

Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Work‑center cost tracking for OEE and labor cost reporting uses a finance cost center; replacing cost_center_code with cost_center_id is standard practice.

Foreign key linking to finance.fixed_asset. Business justification: Work centers (robots, welding equipment, CNC machines) are capitalized as fixed assets. Linking work_center to fixed_asset enables asset lifecycle management, depreciation cost allocation to productio

Foreign key linking to manufacturing.plant. Business justification: A work center belongs to a specific manufacturing plant. The plant_code STRING is a denormalized reference. Adding plant_id FK directly connects work_center to its plant, enabling plant-level work cen

Foreign key linking to inventory.storage_location. Business justification: Work centers have associated lineside storage locations where components are staged for JIT consumption. This link enables shop floor logistics planning, AGV routing to work center supply points, and

business_glossary_term: Automated Guided Vehicle (AGV) Integration Enabled Flag
description: Indicates whether this work center is integrated with AGV (Automated Guided Vehicle) systems for automated material delivery and WIP transport. Drives AGV routing configuration in the MES.

Degree of automation at this work center: fully_automated (no human intervention), highly_automated (minimal human oversight), semi_automated (human-assisted), manual (entirely human-operated), or collaborative (human-robot collaboration). Informs CapEx planning and Industry 4.0 transformation roadmap.
Total available production hours per day for this work center, accounting for planned shift schedules and scheduled breaks. Used in SAP PP capacity requirements planning (CRP) and MES scheduling.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP/CM Module; IATF 16949 Clause 7.1.3

description: Capacity classification of the work center: bottleneck (constraining throughput of the line), standard (normal capacity), buffer (excess capacity used to absorb variation), or flexible (can be redeployed across lines). Critical for Theory of Constraints analysis and production scheduling.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP/CM Module; IATF 16949; SAE International

description: Date on which this work center was officially commissioned and made available for production use. Used for asset age tracking, depreciation calculation, and maintenance lifecycle management.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PM/AA Module; IATF 16949; ISO 9001

description: Timestamp when this work center master record was first created in the data platform. Used for data lineage, audit trail, and record lifecycle management.
ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the cost rates (machine_rate_per_hour, labor_rate_per_hour) associated with this work center (e.g., USD, EUR, JPY).
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; SAP S/4HANA FI/CO Module

description: Standard cycle time in seconds for this work center to complete one unit of work. Used in production scheduling, capacity calculation, and bottleneck analysis. Represents the designed throughput rate per station.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module (Standard Value Key); IATF 16949; SAE International

description: Date on which this work center was or is planned to be permanently decommissioned and removed from production service. Nullable for active stations. Used for asset lifecycle planning and capacity forecasting.

Rated energy consumption of this work center in kilowatts (kW) under standard operating conditions. Used for energy management, sustainability reporting, and ISO 14001 environmental compliance tracking.
reference: ISO 14001 Environmental Management; ISO 50001 Energy Management; EPA Emissions Standards

description: Physical floor area occupied by this work center in square meters. Used for facility layout planning, ergonomic assessments, and plant capacity management.

Indicates whether this work center has been identified as a production bottleneck constraining overall line throughput. Bottleneck stations receive priority in capacity planning, maintenance scheduling, and continuous improvement initiatives.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module; IATF 16949; Theory of Constraints (TOC)

description: Indicates whether this work center participates in JIS (Just-in-Sequence) production sequencing. When true, the MES enforces strict vehicle sequence delivery to this station to support mixed-model assembly.

Indicates whether this work center serves as a quality gate (inspection checkpoint) in the production flow. Quality gate stations require SPC (Statistical Process Control) data capture and may hold WIP pending quality approval.
reference: IATF 16949 Clause 8.5.1; SAP S/4HANA QM Module; Apriso/Dassault MES

description: Standard direct labor cost rate per hour (in local currency) for this work center. Used in production order costing, standard cost roll-up, and labor efficiency variance reporting.
Date of the most recent calibration or measurement system verification performed on this work center's tooling and gauges. Required for IATF 16949 Clause 7.1.5 measurement system compliance.
reference: IATF 16949 Clause 7.1.5; SAP S/4HANA QM Module; ISO 9001 Clause 7.1.5

description: Timestamp of the most recent update to this work center master record. Used for change tracking, data freshness monitoring, and incremental ETL processing in the Databricks lakehouse.
Textual description of the physical location of the work center within the plant (e.g., 'Building A, Bay 3, Row 5'). Supports maintenance routing, safety inspections, and shop floor navigation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PM Module; IATF 16949 Clause 7.1.3

description: Standard machine cost rate per hour (in local currency) for this work center, used in production order costing, standard cost calculation, and variance analysis in SAP CO.

Maintenance strategy applied to this work center: preventive (scheduled interval-based), predictive (condition-monitoring-based), corrective (repair after failure), or condition_based (triggered by sensor thresholds). Drives SAP PM maintenance planning.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PM Module; ISO 14224; IATF 16949 Clause 7.1.3

description: Standard mean time between failures (MTBF) in hours for this work center, representing the expected average operational time between unplanned breakdowns. Used in reliability analysis, maintenance planning, and OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness) benchmarking.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PM Module; ISO 14224; IATF 16949; SAE International

description: Standard mean time to repair (MTTR) in hours for this work center, representing the average time required to restore the station to operational status after a failure. Used in maintenance planning and availability calculations.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PM Module; ISO 14224; IATF 16949

business_glossary_term: Manufacturing Execution System (MES) Work Center ID
description: Native work center identifier in the Apriso/Dassault MES system. Used for cross-system traceability between the lakehouse silver layer and the operational MES for shop floor execution and WIP (Work in Progress) tracking.
Scheduled date for the next calibration or measurement system verification at this work center. Triggers maintenance work orders in SAP PM and quality alerts in the MES when approaching or overdue.
reference: IATF 16949 Clause 7.1.5; SAP S/4HANA QM/PM Module; ISO 9001 Clause 7.1.5

description: Number of production shifts per day this work center operates (typically 1, 2, or 3). Used in daily capacity calculation and workforce planning.

description: Manufacturing process category to which this work center belongs, reflecting the major shop segment (e.g., stamping, body shop, paint, final assembly, powertrain). description: Code identifying the production line (e.g., stamping, body shop, paint, final assembly) to which this work center belongs. Supports JIS (Just-in-Sequence) scheduling and MES line-level reporting.

description: Native work center identifier in SAP S/4HANA PP module. Used for cross-system reconciliation between the lakehouse and SAP for production order routing, capacity planning, and cost center allocation.

business_glossary_term: Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition (SCADA) Node ID
description: Identifier of the SCADA (Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition) node associated with this work center. Enables real-time monitoring, alarm management, and production data acquisition from the shop floor.

description: Standard setup/changeover time in minutes required to prepare this work center for a new production run or model variant. Used in SAP PP capacity planning and MES work order scheduling.

description: Code referencing the shift schedule model assigned to this work center (e.g., 1-shift, 2-shift, 3-shift, weekend shift). Determines available production windows for capacity planning and MES scheduling.

description: SAP PP standard value key (e.g., SAP1, SAP2) defining which standard values (setup, machine, labor, teardown times) are relevant for this work center in routing operations. Governs how production order times are calculated.

description: Takt time in seconds representing the rate at which vehicles must be produced to meet customer demand. Calculated as available production time divided by customer demand rate. Used for JIS (Just-in-Sequence) line balancing and MES pacing.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module; IATF 16949; SAE International J4000

description: Standard teardown/cleanup time in minutes required after completing a production run at this work center. Included in total station time calculations for capacity planning.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module (Standard Value Key); IATF 16949

description: SAP PP work center category classifying the primary resource type: machine (machine-driven capacity), labor (labor-driven capacity), machine_labor (both machine and labor capacity), or external (subcontracted operation). Determines how capacity and costing are calculated in SAP.

description: Externally-known alphanumeric code that uniquely identifies the work center across MES, SAP PP, and SCADA systems. Used as the business key in cross-system integration (e.g., Apriso/Dassault MES work center identifier).

description: Detailed textual description of the work center's function, scope of operations, and any special capabilities or constraints relevant to production planning and scheduling.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module; IATF 16949 Clause 7.1.3

description: Human-readable name of the work center station (e.g., 'Body Framing Station 04', 'Final Torque Assembly Line 2'). Used in shop floor displays, MES dashboards, and production reporting.

description: Current lifecycle status of the work center: active (in production use), inactive (temporarily not in use), under_maintenance (scheduled or unscheduled maintenance), decommissioned (permanently retired), or planned (not yet commissioned). Drives MES scheduling eligibility and capacity planning.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module; IATF 16949 Clause 7.1.3

description: Classification of the work center by its operational mode: manual (human-operated), automated (machine-driven without robotics), robotic (robot arm/AGV-integrated), semi_automated (human-assisted automation), inspection (quality gate), or rework (defect correction station). Drives capacity planning and labor allocation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module; IATF 16949 Clause 7.1.3; SAE International

description: Unique surrogate identifier for a single line-item record within a manufacturing Bill of Materials (BOM). Serves as the primary key for this entity in the Databricks Silver Layer.
