description: Foreign key linking to quality.characteristic. Business justification: Each BOM component has associated quality characteristics (dimensional, functional, safety) per IATF 16949. Linking component to characteristic enables component-level quality control planning and sup

Foreign key linking to supply.inbound_part. Business justification: Each BOM component maps to a specific supplier inbound part for MRP calloff generation, PPAP status tracking per component, and supplier corrective action traceability. supplier_part_number is a denor
Foreign key linking to vehicle.model. Business justification: Component Specification Process tracks components per vehicle model, enabling model‑specific quality and cost analysis.

Foreign key linking to engineering.part_master. Business justification: BOM components need a direct link to part_master for detailed specs; part_number/description are denormalized and will be removed.
Reference to the manufacturing plant or assembly facility where this BOM component is consumed. Determines plant-specific BOM validity and material availability.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Organizational Structure; IATF 16949

description: Reference to the parent BOM header to which this component line-item belongs. Links the component to its governing vehicle or sub-assembly BOM structure.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM-BOM; Siemens Teamcenter PLM BOM Management

description: Foreign key linking to inventory.sku_master. Business justification: Each BOM component must map to an inventory SKU for procurement planning, backflushing, and MRP explosion. This is a foundational link in automotive manufacturing — component-level inventory managemen

Foreign key linking to inventory.storage_location. Business justification: BOM components are staged at specific lineside supply areas / storage locations for JIT/JIS delivery to assembly stations. supply_area is a denormalized storage location reference. This FK enables lin
Reference to the shop floor work center (e.g., stamping, body shop, paint, final assembly station) where this component is installed or consumed during vehicle build.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Work Center; Apriso/Dassault MES Shop Floor Execution

description: Code identifying the alternate item group to which this component belongs in the BOM. Components within the same alternate group are interchangeable. Used in SAP BOM alternative item management for production flexibility.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM-BOM Alternative Item Group

description: OEM part number of an approved alternate or substitute component that can be used in place of the primary part when the primary is unavailable. Supports supply chain continuity and JIT material substitution.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Substitute Materials; PTC Windchill Parts Classification

description: Indicates whether this component is backflushed (automatically goods-issued upon production order confirmation) rather than requiring a discrete goods movement transaction. True = backflush; False = manual goods issue required. Common for bulk and low-value components.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Backflushing; Apriso/Dassault MES

description: Hierarchical depth of this component within the multi-level BOM structure. Level 0 is the finished vehicle; level 1 is a major assembly; deeper levels represent sub-assemblies and individual parts. Used for BOM explosion and cost rollup.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Multi-Level BOM; Siemens Teamcenter PLM BOM Management

description: Flags this component as a bulk material (e.g., adhesives, lubricants, fasteners) that is issued to the production order without discrete picking or goods movement. Bulk materials are typically excluded from MRP individual requirements.

description: Flags this BOM item as a co-product or by-product that is produced alongside the primary output (e.g., stamping offcuts, recycled materials). Co-products receive a portion of the production order cost allocation.

Procurement commodity classification code grouping this component into a spend category (e.g., 'ELEC-ECU', 'BODY-STAMPING', 'POWERTRAIN-EV-BATTERY'). Used in supply chain management (SCM), RFQ processes, and supplier performance reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Material Group; PTC Windchill Parts Classification; SCM; RFQ

description: Current lifecycle status of this BOM component line. 'superseded' indicates the part has been replaced by an alternate; 'pending_approval' indicates the component is awaiting PPAP (Production Part Approval Process) sign-off before use in production.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Material Status; IATF 16949 PPAP; APQP

description: Classification of the BOM item indicating how it is sourced and consumed in the manufacturing process. 'phantom' denotes a logical grouping with no physical stock; 'bulk_material' denotes items issued without discrete picking. reference: SAP S/4HANA MM BOM Item Category; Siemens Teamcenter PLM

description: Timestamp when this BOM component record was first created in the source system (SAP or Teamcenter PLM). Provides audit trail for BOM change history and regulatory traceability.
reference: IATF 16949 Clause 7.5 Documented Information; SAP S/4HANA Change Documents

description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the standard cost value (e.g., 'USD', 'EUR', 'JPY'). Enables multi-currency BOM cost analysis across global manufacturing plants.
reference: ISO 4217; SAP S/4HANA FI Currency; EBITDA

description: Reference to the CAD/CAE engineering drawing or 3D model document number associated with this component, as managed in CATIA/ENOVIA or PTC Windchill. Enables traceability from the manufacturing BOM back to the design definition.

description: Revision level of the engineering drawing or CAD model applicable to this BOM component (e.g., 'A', 'B', '01'). Ensures the correct design revision is used in production and supports PPAP documentation.
reference: Dassault Systèmes CATIA/ENOVIA; PTC Windchill; IATF 16949 PPAP

description: Date after which this BOM component line is no longer valid and should be excluded from production builds. Null indicates an open-ended validity. Supports EOP (End of Production) transitions and engineering change cutover.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM-BOM Validity; Siemens Teamcenter PLM Engineering Change; EOP

description: Date from which this BOM component line is valid and should be included in production builds. Supports engineering change management and model year (MY) transitions. Components before this date are not called off.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM-BOM Validity; Siemens Teamcenter PLM Engineering Change; SOP

description: Reference to the Engineering Change Notice (ECN) or Engineering Change Order (ECO) that introduced or last modified this BOM component line. Provides full traceability of design changes from PLM through to the manufacturing BOM.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM Engineering Change; PTC Windchill Engineering Data Management; IATF 16949 Clause 8.3.6

business_glossary_term: Failure Mode and Effects Analysis (FMEA) Reference
description: Document reference number for the Design FMEA (DFMEA) or Process FMEA (PFMEA) associated with this component. Supports APQP (Advanced Product Quality Planning) traceability and IATF 16949 risk management requirements.
reference: IATF 16949 Clause 8.3.3; FMEA; APQP; SAE J1739

description: Flags this component as containing hazardous materials subject to regulatory controls (e.g., lead, cadmium, hexavalent chromium under EU ELV Directive, or lithium battery cells under UN 38.3). Triggers special handling, storage, and disposal procedures.
reference: EU ELV Directive 2000/53/EC; EU RoHS Directive; UNECE; EPA; DOT FMVSS

description: Numeric sequence defining the order in which this component is installed relative to other components at the same work center. Critical for JIS (Just-in-Sequence) material call-offs and shop floor assembly instruction generation.

description: SAP BOM item category code indicating how the component is managed in inventory and procurement (e.g., stock item 'L', non-stock item 'N', variable-size item 'R'). Drives material requirements planning (MRP) and goods movement behavior.

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to this BOM component record in the source system. Used for incremental data loading, change detection, and BOM audit trail compliance.
reference: IATF 16949 Clause 7.5 Documented Information; SAP S/4HANA Change Documents

description: Number of days before or after the parent order start date that this component must be available at the work center. Negative values indicate the component must arrive before the order start. Used in JIT/JIS scheduling and MRP net requirements calculation.

description: Four-digit vehicle model year (MY) for which this BOM component is applicable (e.g., '2025'). Used to filter BOM components during model year changeover, SOP (Start of Production) planning, and regulatory homologation.

description: Four-digit SAP routing operation number to which this BOM component is assigned. Links the component to the specific assembly step in the production routing, enabling shop floor assembly instruction generation and MES work order sequencing.

description: Indicates whether this component is a phantom assembly — a logical grouping used for BOM structuring that has no physical stock or production order. MRP explodes through phantom assemblies to plan the underlying components directly.

description: Current PPAP approval status for this component, indicating whether the supplier has received production part approval from the OEM quality team. Components without approved PPAP status cannot be used in series production. Aligns with IATF 16949 requirements.
reference: IATF 16949 Clause 8.4.1; PPAP; APQP; SAP S/4HANA QM

description: Numeric priority rank of this component within its alternate item group. Lower numbers indicate higher preference. MRP uses this priority to determine which alternate to consume first when multiple substitutes are available.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM-BOM Alternative Item Priority; MRP

description: Number of units of this component required to build one complete vehicle or parent assembly. Supports fractional quantities for bulk materials (e.g., 0.5 litres of adhesive). Used in MRP material call-off calculations and JIT/JIS scheduling.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM-BOM Component Quantity; MRP; JIT/JIS

business_glossary_term: REACH Substance of Very High Concern (SVHC) Indicator

description: Expected percentage of this component that will be scrapped during the manufacturing process, expressed as a decimal percentage (e.g., 2.5000 = 2.5%). Used to inflate the planned component quantity in MRP to account for anticipated process losses. Supports PPM (Parts Per Million) defect tracking.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM-BOM Scrap Factor; IATF 16949 Clause 10.2; PPM

Foreign key linking to quality.inspection_plan. Business justification: Each build stage references an inspection plan defining what to inspect at that stage. Existing 'quality_inspection_plan_code' is denormalized. Proper FK enables stage-level quality inspection plannin

description: Timestamp when this build stage record was first created in the system. Provides audit trail for master data governance and change history tracking per IATF 16949 documented information control requirements.

description: The date from which this build stage definition record is valid and applicable for production use. Supports temporal versioning of the build stage master, enabling historical traceability of process changes and engineering change management.

description: The date until which this build stage definition record is valid. Null indicates the record is currently active with no planned expiry. Used for managing engineering changes, process revisions, and model-year transitions in the build stage master.

description: Standard energy consumption in kilowatt-hours (kWh) per vehicle unit processed through this build stage. description: Classification of hazardous materials present or used at this build stage per DOT and EPA regulations (e.g., paint solvents, E-Coat chemicals, welding fumes). Drives EHS (Environmental Health and Safety) compliance, PPE requirements, and ISO 14001 environmental management reporting.

business_glossary_term: IATF (International Automotive Task Force) Process Category
description: IATF 16949 process category designation for this build stage: special-characteristic (processes producing special product/process characteristics requiring enhanced control), standard-process, critical-process (safety-critical per FMVSS), or monitoring-only (observation without active control). description: Indicates whether this build stage is designated as a quality gate checkpoint where vehicles must pass defined quality criteria before advancing to the next stage. Quality gates are mandatory hold points per IATF 16949 and APQP (Advanced Product Quality Planning) requirements.

description: Indicates whether vehicles failing quality checks at this build stage are eligible for rework and re-entry into the production flow, as opposed to being routed to scrap or offline repair. Drives rework routing logic in MES and quality management workflows.

description: The standard number of direct labor operators assigned to this build stage per shift under normal production conditions. Used for workforce planning, capacity balancing, and labor cost standard costing in SAP S/4HANA CO.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP — Standard Value Key; SuccessFactors Workforce Planning

description: Maximum allowable ambient or process temperature in degrees Celsius for this build stage. Exceeding this threshold triggers a quality hold and SCADA alarm. Particularly relevant for paint curing ovens, E-Coat baths, and weld quality control.
reference: IATF 16949 Clause 8.5.1; SAP S/4HANA QM — Inspection Characteristics; SCADA

description: The native stage identifier as defined in the Apriso/Dassault MES system. Used for cross-system reconciliation between the lakehouse silver layer and the operational MES, ensuring traceability of production events back to the source system of record.
reference: Apriso/Dassault MES — Shop Floor Execution; IATF 16949 Clause 8.5.2 — Traceability

description: Minimum allowable ambient or process temperature in degrees Celsius for this build stage to operate within specification. reference: IATF 16949 Clause 8.5.1 — Control of Production Processes; SAP S/4HANA QM

description: Comma-separated list of MY (Model Year) designations for which this build stage definition is applicable (e.g., MY2024,MY2025) or ALL if applicable across all model years. Supports model-year-specific production routing and engineering change management.

description: Alphanumeric code identifying the specific plant area or shop within the manufacturing facility where this build stage is physically located (e.g., SH01 for Stamping Hall 1, BS02 for Body Shop 2). Aligns with SAP S/4HANA work center and plant layout master data.

description: Identifier of the PLC (Programmable Logic Controller) program controlling the automated equipment at this build stage. Used for change management, version control of control programs, and traceability of equipment configuration changes per IATF 16949 requirements.

business_glossary_term: PPM (Parts Per Million) Defect Rate Target

description: Stage code of the immediately preceding build stage in the standard production sequence. Defines the upstream dependency for production flow control, WIP (Work in Progress) tracking, and JIS (Just-in-Sequence) scheduling. Null for the first stage in the sequence.

description: The physical or functional manufacturing area where this build stage is executed (e.g., Stamping Plant, Body Shop, Paint Shop, General Assembly, Chassis Line, PDI Bay). Maps to plant layout zones and is used for capacity planning and SCADA (Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition) integration. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Stamping|Body Shop|Paint Shop|E-Coat|General Assembly|Chassis Line|Trim Line|PDI Bay|Quality Gate|Logistics — promote to reference product]
reference: Apriso/Dassault MES — Shop Floor Execution; SAP S/4HANA PP Work Center

description: Safety risk classification of this build stage per DOT and OSHA standards: standard (routine operations), elevated-risk (requires additional PPE or safety protocols), critical-safety (safety-critical operations with mandatory sign-off), or restricted-access (limited to certified personnel only).
reference: DOT — Federal Motor Vehicle Safety Standards (FMVSS); OSHA 29 CFR 1910; ISO 9001 Clause 8.5

description: The SAP S/4HANA PP routing operation code corresponding to this build stage. Enables direct linkage between the build stage reference master and SAP production order routing operations for cost allocation, scheduling, and confirmation.

description: Integer defining the sequential position of this build stage within the overall vehicle assembly process flow. Used to enforce JIS (Just-in-Sequence) ordering, production line scheduling, and WIP (Work in Progress) traceability. Lower values indicate earlier stages.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module — Production Order Routing; IATF 16949 Clause 8.5.1

description: Standard shift pattern under which this build stage operates: single (one shift per day), double (two shifts), triple (three shifts), or continuous (24/7 operation). Used for capacity planning, labor scheduling in SuccessFactors, and production throughput calculations.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP — Shift Sequence; SuccessFactors Workforce Planning

description: The SOP (Start of Production) date on which this build stage was first commissioned and became operational for regular production. Used for asset lifecycle tracking, warranty baseline calculations, and production history reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP — Production Version; Siemens Teamcenter PLM — Program Milestones

description: Indicates whether SPC (Statistical Process Control) monitoring is active for this build stage. When true, process parameters are continuously monitored against control limits and deviations trigger automated alerts in the MES and SAP QM systems per IATF 16949 requirements.
reference: IATF 16949 Clause 8.5.1 — SPC; AIAG SPC Reference Manual; SAP S/4HANA QM

description: Short alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the build stage within the manufacturing process (e.g., STAMP, BODY, ECOAT, PAINT, TRIM1, CHAS, TRIM2, FINAL, PDI). Used as the business key in MES (Manufacturing Execution System) integrations and production reporting.
reference: Apriso/Dassault MES — Shop Floor Execution; IATF 16949 Clause 8.5

description: Detailed narrative description of the manufacturing activities, operations, and objectives performed at this build stage. Provides context for production engineers, quality auditors, and training documentation. Sourced from PLM and MES configuration documentation.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM — Process Description; IATF 16949 Clause 7.5 — Documented Information

description: Full descriptive name of the build stage as used in production planning and reporting (e.g., Stamping, Body Shop, E-Coat, Paint, Trim-1, Chassis, Trim-2, Final Assembly, PDI — Pre-Delivery Inspection). Displayed in MES dashboards and production schedules.
reference: Apriso/Dassault MES — Shop Floor Execution; SAP S/4HANA PP Module

description: Current lifecycle status of the build stage definition: active (in production use), inactive (temporarily not in use), pilot (under validation/trial), suspended (halted due to quality or safety issue), or decommissioned (permanently retired from the process). Controls whether the stage is available for new production orders.
reference: Apriso/Dassault MES — Stage Configuration; IATF 16949 Clause 7.5

description: Categorical classification of the build stage by its primary manufacturing function: assembly (joining components), fabrication (forming/stamping metal), coating (E-Coat/paint application), inspection (quality gate checks), testing (functional/NVH testing), or logistics (material staging/sequencing).
reference: IATF 16949 Clause 8.5; SAP S/4HANA PP — Production Process Classification

description: The engineered standard cycle time for this build stage measured in seconds. Represents the designed takt-aligned duration for one vehicle unit to complete the stage under normal operating conditions. Used for production scheduling, OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness) calculation, and bottleneck analysis.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP — Standard Value Key; IATF 16949 Clause 8.5.1; SAE International J4000

description: Stage code of the immediately succeeding build stage in the standard production sequence. Defines the downstream handoff point for production flow control and vehicle build traceability. Null for the final stage (PDI) in the sequence.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP — Routing Sequence; Apriso/Dassault MES — Stage Dependency

description: The customer-demand-driven takt time for this build stage in seconds, calculated as available production time divided by required production volume. Defines the rhythm of production and is used to balance line capacity and identify over/under-capacity stages.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP — Capacity Planning; IATF 16949 Clause 8.5.1; SAE International

description: Timestamp of the most recent update to this build stage record. Used for change detection in ETL pipelines, audit trail maintenance, and engineering change management workflows per IATF 16949 documented information control.
reference: IATF 16949 Clause 7.5 — Documented Information; ISO 9001 Clause 7.5

description: SAP S/4HANA work center identifier associated with this build stage. Links the build stage to capacity planning, cost center allocation, and production order routing in the ERP system. Enables integration between MES stage execution and SAP PP/CO modules.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP — Work Center Master (CR01/CR02); IATF 16949 Clause 8.5

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each material consumption (goods issue) transaction record on the shop floor. Primary key of this table.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module – Goods Movement

description: Foreign key linking to manufacturing.manufacturing_bom_component. Business justification: Material consumption records capture actual goods issues against specific BOM components. The bom_item_number STRING is a denormalized reference to the manufacturing_bom_component. Adding manufacturin

Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Material consumption postings (goods issues to production) generate FI documents against specific GL accounts (raw material consumption, WIP inventory accounts). SAP MM-FI integration mandates GL acco

Foreign key linking to supply.inbound_part. Business justification: Material consumption reports must reference the specific inbound part receipt to calculate usage rates and supplier performance metrics.

Foreign key linking to quality.inspection_lot. Business justification: Material consumption references the inspection lot for the consumed batch/component. Existing 'quality_inspection_lot' is denormalized. Proper FK enables incoming material quality traceability during
Reference to the manufacturing plant where the material consumption occurred. Aligns with SAP organizational unit for plant.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM – Plant Organizational Unit

description: Reference to the production order against which this material was consumed. Links consumption to the manufacturing work order in SAP PP.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module – Production Order

description: Reference to the material master record for the consumed part or component. Corresponds to the SAP Material Master (MM) object.
Reference to the storage location (bin, supermarket, line-side buffer) from which the material was issued for consumption.

Foreign key linking to vehicle.vin_registry. Business justification: Material consumption traceability per VIN is a regulatory requirement under IATF 16949 and NHTSA recall regulations — manufacturers must identify which specific components were consumed in building ea
Reference to the work center (assembly station, stamping cell, paint booth, etc.) on the shop floor where the material was consumed.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP – Work Center; Apriso/Dassault MES

description: Code identifying the specific assembly station or line position (JIS – Just-in-Sequence station) where the material was consumed. More granular than work center; used for shop floor traceability.
Supplier or internal batch/lot number of the consumed material. Critical for traceability, recall management, and IATF 16949 batch traceability requirements. Nullable for non-batch-managed materials.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM – Batch Management (CHARG); IATF 16949 Clause 8.5.2

description: Version or revision of the BOM used at the time of consumption. Supports engineering change management and ensures consumption is reconciled against the correct BOM revision.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM – BOM Version; SAP S/4HANA PP – Alternative BOM

description: Current lifecycle status of the material consumption record. 'posted' = goods issue confirmed in SAP; 'reversed' = goods issue reversed (movement type 262); 'blocked' = held for quality review; 'pending' = awaiting MES confirmation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM – Goods Movement Status; Apriso/Dassault MES

description: The precise date and time when the material was physically issued from stock and consumed at the work center on the shop floor. This is the principal business event timestamp for this transaction.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM – Posting Date/Time; Apriso/Dassault MES

description: Monetary value of the consumption variance (actual cost minus planned cost) in plant currency. Positive = cost overrun; negative = cost saving. Key metric for production cost variance reporting in SAP CO.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO – Production Variance (KKBC_ORD); IATF 16949

description: Timestamp when this material consumption record was first created in the source system (SAP MM or MES). Audit trail field for data lineage and Silver layer ingestion tracking.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM – Creation Timestamp; IATF 16949 Record Retention

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the material cost amount (e.g., USD, EUR, JPY). Supports multi-currency manufacturing operations across global plants.
reference: ISO 4217 – Currency Codes; SAP S/4HANA FI

description: Externally visible SAP material document number generated when the goods issue (movement type 261) is posted. Serves as the business-facing transaction identifier traceable in SAP MM.

SAP movement type code indicating the nature of the goods movement (e.g., 261 = goods issue to production order, 262 = reversal, 291 = goods issue to cost center). Drives inventory and cost accounting postings.
Timestamp of the most recent modification to this material consumption record in the source system. Used for incremental data load detection and audit trail in the Databricks Silver layer.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM – Change Timestamp; Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer

description: Standard cost value of the consumed material quantity at the time of goods issue, expressed in the plant currency. Calculated as quantity consumed × standard price from SAP Material Master. Feeds production cost accounting and variance reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO – Standard Cost; FI Goods Issue Valuation

description: Unique transaction identifier from the Apriso/Dassault MES system that triggered or confirmed this material consumption event. Enables cross-system reconciliation between MES shop floor execution and SAP MM goods movements.
reference: Apriso/Dassault MES – Transaction ID; SAP S/4HANA MM Integration

description: The model year (MY) of the vehicle being assembled (e.g., '2025'). Used for model-year-level consumption analysis, BOM alignment, and regulatory compliance reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP – Model Year; NHTSA MY Definition

description: The routing operation number within the production order at which the material was consumed (e.g., operation 0010 = body shop, 0020 = paint, 0030 = final assembly). Enables process-step-level consumption analysis.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP – Routing Operation (VORNR); Apriso/Dassault MES

description: Reference to the original SAP material document number that this record reverses. Populated only when reversal_indicator is True. Enables audit trail for reversed consumption transactions.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM – Reversal Reference Document

description: The planned quantity of this material as specified in the Bill of Materials (BOM) for the production order. Used to calculate consumption variance against actual quantity consumed.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP – BOM Component Quantity; Siemens Teamcenter PLM

description: The accounting posting date for the goods issue in SAP, which may differ from the physical consumption timestamp. Used for period-end cost accounting and production variance reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/CO – Posting Date; IATF 16949

description: Powertrain type of the vehicle being assembled: ICE (Internal Combustion Engine), HEV (Hybrid Electric Vehicle), PHEV (Plug-in Hybrid Electric Vehicle), EV (Electric Vehicle), FCEV (Fuel Cell Electric Vehicle). Drives BOM variant selection and consumption expectations.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP – Configuration; CAFE/EPA Powertrain Classification

description: Code identifying the production line (e.g., 'LINE-A', 'TRIM-2', 'PAINT-EAST') where the material was consumed. Used for line-level consumption analysis and capacity planning.
reference: Apriso/Dassault MES – Production Line; SAP S/4HANA PP

description: Actual quantity of the material consumed (goods issued) against the production order. Expressed in the base unit of measure of the material. Core quantitative fact for this transaction.

Difference between actual quantity consumed and planned BOM quantity (actual minus planned). Positive value indicates over-consumption; negative indicates under-consumption. Key input for production variance reporting and cost accounting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO – Production Variance; IATF 16949 SPC

description: Flag indicating whether this consumption record is a reversal (cancellation) of a previously posted goods issue. True = this is a reversal document; False = original consumption posting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM – Reversal Document (STORNO); Goods Movement

description: Flag indicating whether the consumed material was scrapped (True) rather than incorporated into the vehicle assembly. Scrap consumption is tracked separately for waste reporting, IATF 16949 scrap analysis, and environmental compliance.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP – Scrap; IATF 16949 Clause 8.7; ISO 14001

description: Coded reason for material scrap when scrap_indicator is True (e.g., 'DAMAGE', 'DEFECT', 'WRONG_PART', 'PROCESS_LOSS'). Used for scrap root cause analysis and PPM defect rate reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: DAMAGE|DEFECT|WRONG_PART|PROCESS_LOSS|HANDLING_ERROR|SUPPLIER_DEFECT|OTHER — promote to reference product]
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP – Scrap Reason; IATF 16949 Clause 8.7; FMEA

description: Individual serial number of the consumed component where serial number management is active (e.g., ECU, battery module, transmission). Enables unit-level traceability for safety-critical parts. Nullable for non-serialized materials.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM – Serial Number Management; NHTSA Traceability Requirements

description: Production shift during which the material was consumed (DAY, AFTERNOON, NIGHT). Used for shift-level consumption analysis, variance trending, and workforce productivity reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP – Shift; Apriso/Dassault MES

description: Code identifying the operational system of record from which this consumption record originated (SAP_MM = SAP S/4HANA MM goods movement, MES_APRISO = Apriso/Dassault MES, SAP_PP = SAP Production Planning, MANUAL = manual entry). Supports data lineage in the lakehouse.
reference: Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer – Data Lineage; SAP S/4HANA Integration

description: SAP vendor/supplier number of the supplier who provided the consumed material. Used for supplier-level consumption analysis, PPAP traceability, and supply chain variance reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM – Vendor Master (LFA1); IATF 16949 Supplier Management

description: Base unit of measure for the consumed quantity (e.g., EA = each, KG = kilogram, L = litre, M = metre, PC = piece). Aligns with SAP base UoM from Material Master. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|KG|L|M|M2|M3|PC|SET|ROL|BOX — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM – Base Unit of Measure (MEINS); ISO 80000

description: Quantity variance expressed as a percentage of the planned BOM quantity ((actual - planned) / planned × 100). Used for threshold-based alerting and PPM (Parts Per Million) defect rate analysis.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO – Variance Analysis; IATF 16949

description: Internal OEM code identifying the vehicle model or platform being assembled (e.g., 'F150', 'CAMRY-HEV', 'SILVERADO-EV'). Used for model-level consumption aggregation and BOM variance analysis.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP – Material (Finished Vehicle); Siemens Teamcenter PLM

description: Sequential position of this consumption event within the production order's WIP (Work in Progress) processing sequence. Used for JIS (Just-in-Sequence) ordering analysis and shop floor flow traceability.
Unique surrogate identifier for the manufacturing Bill of Materials (mBOM) record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Serves as the primary key for this entity.
reference: SAP PP BOM Header (CS01/CS02); IATF 16949 Clause 8.1

description: Foreign key linking to quality.apqp_plan. Business justification: Production BOMs are released through APQP gates; ppap_status and apqp_phase on production_bom require traceability to the governing APQP plan. IATF 16949 APQP requires BOM-to-APQP-plan linkage for des

Foreign key linking to engineering.part_master. Business justification: The production BOM header references the top-level assembly part (finished vehicle or major subassembly) in part_master — standard SAP MBOM pattern. Required for cost rollup, PPAP submission, and engi

business_glossary_term: Engineering Bill of Materials (eBOM) Reference ID
description: Reference identifier linking this manufacturing BOM (mBOM) to the originating engineering BOM (eBOM) in Siemens Teamcenter PLM or PTC Windchill. Enables traceability from shop floor execution back to the engineering design intent. The eBOM-to-mBOM transformation is managed via the PLM-SAP integration.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM – BOM Management; PTC Windchill EDM; IATF 16949 Clause 8.3

description: Foreign key linking to compliance.homologation_record. Business justification: Production BOMs must reflect homologation-approved component specifications for regulatory markets. Parts substitution or BOM changes require homologation validation. Real business process: homologati

Foreign key linking to supply.inbound_part. Business justification: Production BOM must reference the approved inbound part to support supplier calloff generation, PPAP validation against BOM, and sourcing coverage checks during APQP. Manufacturing engineers and suppl
Foreign key linking to vehicle.model. Business justification: BOM Management Report requires each BOM to be tied to a vehicle model to ensure correct component list per model.

business_glossary_term: Vehicle Model Year Program Id (Foreign Key)
description: Foreign key linking to vehicle.vehicle_model_year_program. Business justification: Production BOMs are versioned and effectivity-dated by model year program. APQP phase gates and PPAP submissions are organized per model year program. This FK enables BOM effectivity management and PP

Foreign key linking to manufacturing.plant. Business justification: A manufacturing BOM is plant-specific — the same vehicle model may have different production BOMs at different plants due to local tooling, supplier sourcing, or process differences. The plant_code ST

Foreign key linking to vehicle.powertrain_variant. Business justification: Production BOMs are powertrain-variant-specific — EV, PHEV, and ICE variants require fundamentally different BOMs. PPAP and APQP processes require BOM validation against the certified powertrain_varia

business_glossary_term: Engineering Bill of Materials (eBOM) Reference ID
description: Reference identifier linking this manufacturing BOM (mBOM) to the originating engineering BOM (eBOM) in Siemens Teamcenter PLM or PTC Windchill. Enables traceability from shop floor execution back to the engineering design intent. The eBOM-to-mBOM transformation is managed via the PLM-SAP integration.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM – BOM Management; PTC Windchill EDM; IATF 16949 Clause 8.3

description: Foreign key linking to manufacturing.production_line. Business justification: A production BOM is specific to a production line (e.g., a stamping line BOM differs from a final assembly BOM). The production_line_code STRING is a denormalized reference. Adding production_line_id

Foreign key linking to manufacturing.routing. Business justification: A production BOM is associated with a specific routing that defines the sequence of operations for manufacturing. The routing_number STRING is a denormalized reference. Adding routing_id FK normalizes

Foreign key linking to inventory.sku_master. Business justification: Production BOMs define the finished assembly material number used for inventory valuation and MRP. material_number is a denormalized SKU reference. This FK is fundamental to SAP PP-MM integration for

The date on which this manufacturing BOM received formal approval from the designated authority. Precedes the release_date in the BOM lifecycle workflow. Required for IATF 16949 documented information control.
reference: IATF 16949 Clause 7.5; SAP PP Workflow; ISO 9001 Clause 7.5

description: User ID or employee identifier of the approving authority (e.g., Chief Engineer, Manufacturing Manager) who approved this BOM for production release. Distinct from released_by — approval may be a separate workflow step in Teamcenter PLM or SAP workflow.
reference: IATF 16949 Clause 8.3.3 – Design and Development Outputs; SAP PP Workflow; ISO 9001 Clause 8.3

description: Current APQP phase associated with this BOM's program lifecycle. Phase 1=Plan and Define, Phase 2=Product Design and Development, Phase 3=Process Design and Development, Phase 4=Product and Process Validation, Phase 5=Launch/Feedback. Tracks BOM maturity relative to the program development gate.
reference: AIAG APQP 2nd Edition; IATF 16949 Clause 8.3; SAP PP Program Management

description: Indicates whether the vehicle is assembled as a Completely Built Unit (CBU), Completely Knocked Down (CKD) kit for overseas assembly, or Semi Knocked Down (SKD) kit. Determines the BOM structure, component packaging, and applicable customs/import regulations for the target market.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP – Production Type; UNECE Vehicle Regulations; Customs Tariff Regulations

description: The base quantity of the header material for which the BOM component quantities are defined (e.g., 1 vehicle). All component quantities in the BOM are expressed relative to this base quantity. Corresponds to the SAP BOM header base quantity field.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP – BOM Header Base Quantity; ISO 9001 Clause 8.1

description: The unit of measure for the BOM base quantity (e.g., 'EA' for each/vehicle, 'PC' for piece, 'SET' for set). Corresponds to the SAP material master base unit of measure. Ensures consistent quantity calculations during MRP explosion and production order processing. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|PC|KG|L|M|M2|M3|SET — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM – Unit of Measure; ISO 80000-1 Quantities and Units

description: SAP BOM alternative identifier (e.g., '01', '02') distinguishing multiple valid BOM configurations for the same material and plant. Supports variant BOMs for different production lines or regional configurations (e.g., LHD vs RHD, CKD vs SKD).
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module – BOM Alternative; IATF 16949 Clause 8.1

description: Human-readable description of the manufacturing BOM, typically including the vehicle model, model year, plant, and key configuration attributes (e.g., '2025 F-150 XLT 3.5L EcoBoost AWD – Detroit Assembly Plant – MY2025 Production BOM'). Used for display in SAP, Teamcenter PLM, and reporting tools.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP – BOM Header Description; Siemens Teamcenter PLM

description: The maximum depth/level of the BOM hierarchy for this manufacturing BOM (e.g., 1 = single-level, 5 = five levels deep). Used to understand BOM complexity and to control multi-level BOM explosion in MRP and production scheduling.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP – Multi-Level BOM; IATF 16949 Clause 8.1

description: Current lifecycle state of the manufacturing BOM. 'draft' indicates the BOM is under engineering review. 'released' means it has been authorized for production use. 'frozen' indicates no further changes are permitted (e.g., during active production run). 'superseded' means a newer BOM version has replaced it. 'obsolete' means the BOM is no longer valid.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module – BOM Status; IATF 16949 Clause 8.1; Siemens Teamcenter PLM BOM Management

description: Classification of the BOM by its intended use in the manufacturing process. 'production' is the standard mBOM used on the shop floor. 'phantom' represents sub-assemblies that are not stocked. 'sales' is used for variant configuration. 'spare_parts' is for after-sales service. 'co_product' is for by-products in manufacturing.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module – BOM Category; IATF 16949 Clause 8.1

description: SAP BOM usage indicator defining the organizational context in which the BOM is applied. 'production' (SAP usage 1) is the standard for shop floor manufacturing. 'engineering' (SAP usage 2) is for design/development. 'universal' (SAP usage 3) applies across all usages. Determines which BOM is selected during MRP and production order processing.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP – BOM Usage (T415); IATF 16949 Clause 8.1

description: Version identifier of the manufacturing BOM (e.g., 'v1.0', 'v2.3'). Incremented each time the BOM is revised through an approved Engineering Change Order. Enables version history tracking and rollback capability for quality investigations.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM – BOM Versioning; IATF 16949 Clause 7.5; SAP PP BOM Change History

description: Free-text description of the business reason for creating or modifying this BOM version (e.g., 'Cost reduction – substitute supplier for part X', 'Regulatory compliance – CARB Tier 3 emission update', 'Quality improvement – NVH fix per TSB-2024-001'). Required for IATF 16949 change control documentation.
reference: IATF 16949 Clause 8.5.6 – Control of Changes; SAP PP Change Master; ISO 9001 Clause 8.5.6

description: Total number of distinct component line items (BOM positions) in this manufacturing BOM. Provides a quick indicator of BOM complexity and is used for completeness validation during BOM release. Does not include phantom sub-assembly components.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP – BOM Item Count; IATF 16949 Clause 8.1

description: Timestamp when this manufacturing BOM record was first created in the source system (SAP PP or Teamcenter PLM). Represents the business event of BOM creation, not the ETL load time. Formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX per enterprise data standards.
reference: IATF 16949 Clause 7.5 – Documented Information; ISO 9001 Clause 7.5; SAP PP BOM Header

description: The Engineering Change Order (ECO) number that authorized the creation or last modification of this manufacturing BOM. Provides full traceability from the shop floor BOM back to the formal engineering change management process in Teamcenter PLM. Required for IATF 16949 change control compliance.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM – Engineering Change Management; IATF 16949 Clause 8.5.6; SAP PP ECO Integration

description: The date after which this manufacturing BOM version is no longer valid for production use. Corresponds to the SAP BOM 'Valid To' date. Null indicates the BOM is open-ended with no planned expiry. Used to manage BOM transitions during model year changeovers and End of Production (EOP).
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP – BOM Effectivity; IATF 16949 Clause 8.1

description: The date from which this manufacturing BOM version becomes effective and valid for production use. Corresponds to the SAP BOM 'Valid From' date. Used for date-effective BOM explosion in MRP and production order creation. Aligns with Start of Production (SOP) planning.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP – BOM Effectivity; IATF 16949 Clause 8.1; SAE J1739

description: The planned or actual End of Production (EOP) date for the vehicle program associated with this BOM. Marks the milestone when serial production of this vehicle configuration ceases. Used for last-time-buy planning, spare parts provisioning, and regulatory compliance archiving.
reference: IATF 16949 Clause 8.1; SAP PP Production Planning; APQP

description: Status of the regulatory homologation (type approval) for the vehicle configuration defined by this BOM in the target regulatory market. 'approved' is required before vehicles built to this BOM can be legally sold in the target market. Managed through the Regulatory Compliance and Homologation process.
reference: NHTSA 49 CFR Part 567 – Certification; UNECE WP.29 Type Approval; Euro NCAP; DOT FMVSS

description: Indicates whether this BOM uses SAP Variant Configuration (VC) to support multiple vehicle variants from a single configurable BOM structure. True = configurable BOM with characteristic-based component selection. False = fixed BOM for a single specific vehicle configuration.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP – Variant Configuration (LO-VC); IATF 16949 Clause 8.1

description: Indicates whether the header material of this BOM is a phantom assembly — a logical grouping of components that is not physically stocked or produced as a discrete unit. Phantom assemblies are 'exploded through' during MRP and production order processing. Common for sub-assemblies in JIS manufacturing.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP – Phantom Assembly (Special Procurement Key 50); IATF 16949 Clause 8.1

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to this manufacturing BOM record in the source system. Used to detect changes during incremental data loads into the Databricks Silver Layer and to support BOM change history auditing required by IATF 16949.
reference: IATF 16949 Clause 7.5 – Documented Information; SAP PP BOM Change History; ISO 9001 Clause 7.5

description: The four-digit automotive Model Year (MY) for which this manufacturing BOM is valid (e.g., 2025). Determines the applicable regulatory standards, feature content, and homologation requirements. Aligns with NHTSA and EPA model year definitions.
reference: NHTSA 49 CFR Part 565 – VIN; SAP PP Material Master; IATF 16949

description: Classification of the vehicle powertrain technology for which this BOM is defined. ICE = Internal Combustion Engine, HEV = Hybrid Electric Vehicle, PHEV = Plug-in Hybrid Electric Vehicle, BEV = Battery Electric Vehicle, FCEV = Fuel Cell Electric Vehicle. Determines applicable emissions regulations (EPA, CARB, WLTP) and BOM component sets.
reference: EPA 40 CFR Part 86 – Emissions Standards; CARB Title 13; CAFE Standards; SAE J1711

description: Status of the Production Part Approval Process (PPAP) for the components in this BOM. PPAP is the AIAG/IATF 16949 process that verifies supplier components meet design and specification requirements before production launch. 'approved' is required before the BOM can be released to production.
reference: AIAG PPAP 4th Edition; IATF 16949 Clause 8.4.1; SAP QM – Quality Notifications

description: BOM number of the previous manufacturing BOM version that this BOM supersedes. Enables backward navigation in the BOM version chain for change impact analysis and quality investigations. Null for the initial BOM version.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP – BOM Change History; IATF 16949 Clause 8.5.6; Siemens Teamcenter PLM

description: Code identifying the target regulatory market for which this BOM is homologated (e.g., 'US-FEDERAL', 'US-CARB', 'EU-EURO6', 'JP-JC08', 'CN-CHINA6'). Determines applicable emissions standards (EPA, CARB, Euro 6, WLTP), safety regulations (NHTSA FMVSS, UNECE), and CAFE compliance requirements.
reference: NHTSA 49 CFR – FMVSS; EPA 40 CFR Part 86; CARB Title 13; Euro NCAP; UNECE WP.29; CAFE Standards

description: The date on which this manufacturing BOM was formally released to production (status changed to 'released'). Marks the point at which the BOM is authorized for use in production orders and MRP planning. Corresponds to the SAP BOM release date.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP – BOM Release; IATF 16949 Clause 8.1; APQP Phase 4

description: User ID or employee identifier of the manufacturing engineer or release authority who formally released this BOM to production in SAP PP or Teamcenter PLM. Required for audit trail and change control compliance under IATF 16949.
reference: IATF 16949 Clause 7.5 – Documented Information; SAP PP Authorization; ISO 9001 Clause 7.5

description: Alphanumeric revision level of the BOM (e.g., 'A', 'B', 'C' or '01', '02'). Used in conjunction with bom_version to track incremental changes within a version. Aligns with SAP BOM revision level and Teamcenter PLM revision management.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP – BOM Revision; Siemens Teamcenter PLM; IATF 16949 Clause 7.5

description: The planned or actual Start of Production (SOP) date for the vehicle program associated with this BOM. Marks the milestone when serial production begins using this BOM configuration. Critical for program management, supplier readiness, and PPAP completion tracking.
reference: APQP Phase 4 – Product and Process Validation; IATF 16949 Clause 8.3; SAP PP Production Planning

description: Identifies the operational system of record from which this manufacturing BOM record was sourced (e.g., 'SAP_PP' for SAP Production Planning, 'TEAMCENTER' for Siemens Teamcenter PLM). Required for data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer and for resolving conflicts during BOM synchronization between PLM and ERP.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP; Siemens Teamcenter PLM; PTC Windchill; Dassault ENOVIA; Apriso MES

description: BOM number of the newer manufacturing BOM that has replaced this one (populated when bom_status = 'superseded'). Enables forward navigation in the BOM version chain for traceability and historical analysis. Null when this is the current active BOM.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP – BOM Change History; IATF 16949 Clause 8.5.6; Siemens Teamcenter PLM

description: Code identifying the specific vehicle configuration (variant) for which this BOM is defined, including body style, drivetrain, trim level, and market variant (e.g., 'SUV-AWD-PREMIUM-NA'). Used in SAP Variant Configuration (VC) to select the correct BOM components for a specific vehicle order.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP – Variant Configuration; Dassault ENOVIA Configuration Management; IATF 16949

description: Internal OEM code identifying the vehicle model (nameplate) for which this mBOM is defined (e.g., 'F150', 'RAV4', 'MODEL3'). Used to associate the BOM with the correct vehicle program in PLM and SAP.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM – Product Structure; SAP PP Material Master

description: Primary key for production_variant

Foreign key linking to quality.apqp_plan. Business justification: Each production variant (market-specific vehicle configuration) has an APQP plan governing its launch quality gates. APQP plans are variant-specific per IATF 16949 APQP requirements; ppap_status on pr

Foreign key linking to quality.control_plan. Business justification: Each production variant is governed by a variant-specific control plan (different markets/configurations have different quality controls). PPAP approval requires a control plan per variant. ppap_statu

Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Each production variant has a standard cost tracked against a cost center for product costing and variant-level profitability analysis. Automotive OEMs assign cost centers to production variants to en

Foreign key linking to engineering.ecu_specification. Business justification: Each production variant requires a specific ECU specification governing software version, calibration dataset, and OTA capability for end-of-line programming and homologation. Manufacturing and softwa
Foreign key linking to compliance.homologation_record. Business justification: Variant definitions are approved via homologation; linking ensures variant builds only when approved.
Foreign key linking to vehicle.model. Business justification: Variant Definition Process links each variant to its vehicle model for configuration management and regulatory compliance.

business_glossary_term: Vehicle Model Year Program Id (Foreign Key)
description: Foreign key linking to vehicle.vehicle_model_year_program. Business justification: Production variants are scoped to model year programs — SOP/EOP dates, regulatory variants, and PPAP status are all model-year-program-specific. Manufacturing variant lifecycle management requires thi

Foreign key linking to sales.msrp_schedule. Business justification: Production variant-to-MSRP alignment is required for margin analysis and launch readiness: manufacturers validate that every production variant has an active published MSRP schedule before SOP. This l
Reference to the manufacturing plant where this production variant is authorized to be built. Determines line compatibility, tooling availability, and regulatory homologation scope.

Foreign key linking to engineering.powertrain_spec. Business justification: Each production variant is governed by a specific engineering powertrain_spec defining assembly parameters, emissions compliance, and PPAP requirements. Manufacturing engineers and APQP teams require

Foreign key linking to vehicle.powertrain_variant. Business justification: Manufacturing production variants must trace to certified vehicle powertrain_variants for emissions compliance validation, PPAP approval, and regulatory homologation. Production engineers verify each

Foreign key linking to billing.billing_price_condition. Business justification: Variant-level pricing drives dealer invoice amounts and MSRP in automotive billing. Each production variant (trim, powertrain, options) maps to specific SAP SD price conditions used to generate the ve

Reference to the manufacturing BOM (Bill of Materials) version applicable to this production variant. Links to the BOM master in Siemens Teamcenter PLM or SAP MM (Materials Management) to resolve component lists for production planning and MRP (Material Requirements Planning) execution.
reference: IATF 16949 Clause 8.3.5 — Design and Development Outputs; SAP MM BOM Management

description: Reference to the specific production line within the plant on which this variant is authorized to be assembled. Drives JIS (Just-in-Sequence) feasibility validation and takt time allocation.
reference: IATF 16949 Clause 8.5 — Production and Service Provision

description: Reference to the manufacturing routing (operation sequence) applicable to this production variant in SAP PP (Production Planning). Defines the sequence of work centers, operations, and standard times required to build this variant from stamping through final assembly.
reference: IATF 16949 Clause 8.5.1 — Control of Production; SAP PP Routing

description: Foreign key linking to inventory.sku_master. Business justification: Each production variant maps to a finished-goods SKU for inventory valuation, dealer stock management, and SAP material master integration. sap_material_number is a denormalized SKU reference that thi

Foreign key linking to vehicle.vehicle_trim_level. Business justification: Production variants are built to specific trim levels, driving component allocation, labor standards, and JIS sequencing. Manufacturing scheduling systems require the trim_level FK to determine correc
Foreign key linking to engineering.vehicle_program. Business justification: Variants are defined under a vehicle program; variant management reports require vehicle_program_id.

business_glossary_term: Advanced Driver Assistance Systems (ADAS) Automation Level
description: SAE J3016 automation level of the ADAS (Advanced Driver Assistance Systems) suite fitted to this production variant: L0 (no automation), L1 (driver assistance), L2 (partial automation), L2_PLUS (enhanced partial automation), L3 (conditional automation). Drives ECU (Electronic Control Unit) software configuration, OTA (Over-the-Air Update) eligibility, and UNECE UN R157 regulatory compliance.
reference: SAE J3016 — Taxonomy of Driving Automation; UNECE UN R157 — ALKS; NHTSA AV Policy

description: Numeric score (1–10) representing the relative assembly complexity of this production variant compared to the base variant on the same production line. Higher scores indicate more assembly operations, longer cycle times, or specialized tooling requirements. Used for JIS (Just-in-Sequence) sequencing optimization to balance line loading and avoid consecutive high-complexity builds.
reference: IATF 16949 Clause 8.5.1; SAE J4000 — Lean Manufacturing Assessment

description: Usable battery pack capacity in kilowatt-hours (kWh) for BEV, PHEV, and HEV variants. Null for pure ICE variants. Drives assembly sequencing for battery marriage station, WLTP (Worldwide Harmonised Light Vehicles Test Procedure) range certification, and EPA electric range labelling.
reference: EPA 40 CFR Part 600 — Fuel Economy; WLTP Regulation (EU) 2017/1151; SAE J1634

description: Standardized code identifying the vehicle body configuration for this production variant (e.g., SEDAN, SUV, TRUCK, COUPE, HATCHBACK, MINIVAN). Determines stamping die sets, body shop tooling, and paint booth configuration requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: SEDAN|SUV|TRUCK|COUPE|HATCHBACK|MINIVAN|CONVERTIBLE|WAGON|VAN|CROSSOVER — promote to reference product]
reference: SAE J1100 — Vehicle Dimensions; ISO 3833 — Road Vehicles Types

description: Certified CO2 tailpipe emission in grams per kilometre (g/km) under WLTP or NEDC test cycle for this production variant. Used for EU CO2 fleet average compliance (Regulation (EU) 2019/631), CAFE (Corporate Average Fuel Economy) calculations, and vehicle taxation band classification in destination markets.
reference: EU Regulation 2019/631 — CO2 Standards for Cars; EPA 40 CFR Part 86; CARB Title 13

description: Timestamp when this production variant record was first created in the manufacturing master data system. Provides audit trail for data governance and IATF 16949 documented information control requirements.
reference: IATF 16949 Clause 7.5 — Documented Information; ISO 9001 Clause 7.5

description: Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code identifying the primary market for which this production variant is homologated and destined (e.g., 'USA', 'DEU', 'JPN'). Governs regulatory compliance requirements (NHTSA FMVSS, Euro NCAP, UNECE), emission standards, and right-hand vs left-hand drive configuration.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3; NHTSA FMVSS; UNECE Vehicle Regulations; Euro NCAP

description: Drivetrain configuration specifying which axles receive motive power: FWD (Front-Wheel Drive), RWD (Rear-Wheel Drive), AWD (All-Wheel Drive), or 4WD (Four-Wheel Drive). Determines chassis assembly routing, driveshaft fitment, and NVH (Noise Vibration Harshness) validation requirements.

Total engine displacement in cubic centimetres (cc) for ICE (Internal Combustion Engine) and HEV/PHEV variants. Null for BEV and FCEV variants. Used for regulatory classification under EPA, CARB, and UNECE emissions standards, and for CAFE (Corporate Average Fuel Economy) fleet averaging calculations.
reference: EPA 40 CFR Part 86; UNECE Regulation No. 83 — Emissions

description: Current engineering change revision level for this production variant as managed in Siemens Teamcenter PLM or PTC Windchill (e.g., 'A', 'B', 'C1', 'REV3'). Tracks design maturity and ensures the correct BOM (Bill of Materials) version is applied during production. Incremented upon each approved engineering change notice (ECN).
reference: IATF 16949 Clause 8.3.6 — Design and Development Changes; Siemens Teamcenter PLM ECM

description: Date on which this production variant is scheduled to cease production (EOP — End of Production). Drives last-order scheduling, tooling decommissioning planning, and spare parts provisioning for after-sales service obligations.
reference: APQP Phase 5 — Feedback, Assessment and Corrective Action; IATF 16949

description: EPA-certified combined fuel economy in miles per gallon (MPG) for ICE, HEV, and PHEV variants in NAFTA markets. Null for BEV/FCEV variants. Used for CAFE (Corporate Average Fuel Economy) fleet compliance reporting to EPA and consumer window sticker labelling under 49 CFR Part 575.
reference: EPA 40 CFR Part 600 — Fuel Economy; CAFE Standards 49 CFR Part 533; NHTSA

description: Gross Vehicle Weight Rating (GVWR) in kilograms for this production variant. Used for regulatory classification under DOT FMVSS, UNECE weight regulations, and CAFE (Corporate Average Fuel Economy) fleet averaging. Also drives chassis assembly torque specifications and suspension tuning parameters.
reference: NHTSA FMVSS 120; UNECE Regulation No. 13 — Braking; DOT 49 CFR Part 571

description: Steering wheel position for this production variant: LHD (Left-Hand Drive) for markets such as USA, Germany; RHD (Right-Hand Drive) for markets such as UK, Japan, Australia. Determines instrument panel assembly variant, steering column routing, and body shop tooling configuration.
reference: UNECE Regulation No. 48 — Installation of Lighting; ISO 4130

description: Regulatory type-approval and homologation status for this production variant in the target destination market: PENDING (application submitted), CERTIFIED (full approval granted), CONDITIONAL (provisional approval with conditions), EXPIRED (certification lapsed), NOT_REQUIRED. Governs whether the variant can be legally sold in the destination market.
reference: UNECE WP.29 — Type Approval; NHTSA FMVSS Certification; Euro NCAP; DOT 49 CFR Part 567

description: Indicates whether this production variant can be produced as a CKD (Completely Knocked Down) kit for assembly at an overseas plant (True = CKD eligible). Drives export documentation, customs tariff classification, and overseas plant BOM (Bill of Materials) configuration in SAP MM.
reference: WTO Customs Valuation Agreement; SAP MM CKD Processing; IATF 16949

description: Indicates whether this production variant is eligible for JIS (Just-in-Sequence) supplier call-off sequencing (True = JIS eligible). JIS-eligible variants require sequenced component delivery synchronized to the build sequence, impacting supplier logistics contracts and MES call-off trigger configuration.
reference: IATF 16949 Clause 8.4 — Control of Externally Provided Processes; SAE J4000

description: Indicates whether this production variant has been validated as feasible for assembly on the assigned production line (True = compatible, False = not compatible). Serves as the primary gate flag in MES (Manufacturing Execution System) build sequence feasibility validation and JIS (Just-in-Sequence) call-off authorization.
reference: IATF 16949 Clause 8.5.1 — Control of Production; Apriso/Dassault MES

description: Indicates whether this production variant supports OTA (Over-the-Air) software updates for ECU (Electronic Control Unit) and infotainment systems (True = OTA capable). Drives connected vehicle service provisioning in Geotab/Bosch IoT platform and UNECE UN R156 software update regulatory compliance.
reference: UNECE UN R156 — Software Update Processes; NHTSA Cybersecurity Best Practices; SAE J3101

description: Maximum number of consecutive units of this production variant that can be scheduled back-to-back in the build sequence without violating line balancing or ergonomic constraints. Enforced by MES (Manufacturing Execution System) JIS (Just-in-Sequence) sequencing rules to prevent operator fatigue and tooling overload.
reference: IATF 16949 Clause 8.5.1 — Control of Production; Apriso/Dassault MES Sequencing Rules

description: Cross-reference identifier for this production variant in the Apriso/Dassault MES (Manufacturing Execution System). Used for real-time build sequence call-off, WIP (Work in Progress) tracking, and quality gate routing on the shop floor.
reference: Apriso/Dassault MES — Shop Floor Execution; IATF 16949 Clause 8.5.1

description: Four-digit model year (MY) for which this production variant is defined and authorized. Governs BOM (Bill of Materials) version applicability, regulatory homologation, and CAFE (Corporate Average Fuel Economy) compliance grouping. Range: 2000–2040.
reference: NHTSA 49 CFR Part 565 — VIN Requirements; EPA CAFE Standards

business_glossary_term: New Car Assessment Programme (NCAP) Safety Rating
description: Overall NCAP (New Car Assessment Programme) safety star rating achieved by this production variant (1 to 5 stars, or NOT_RATED if not yet assessed). Applicable to Euro NCAP, NHTSA NCAP, or ANCAP depending on destination market. Used for regulatory compliance reporting and marketing communications.
reference: Euro NCAP Assessment Protocol; NHTSA 5-Star Safety Ratings Program; ANCAP

description: Code identifying the bundled option package associated with this production variant (e.g., 'TECH_PKG', 'SAFETY_PKG', 'LUXURY_PKG'). Maps to a specific set of BOM (Bill of Materials) components and assembly operations in Siemens Teamcenter PLM and SAP PP configurable material management.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM — Variant Configuration; SAP PP — Configurable Materials (KMAT)

description: Classification of the paint application process required for this production variant: STANDARD (solid colour), METALLIC, PEARL, MATTE, or TWO_TONE. Determines paint booth routing, cure cycle parameters, and paint material BOM (Bill of Materials) components in the body shop and paint shop assembly stages.
reference: IATF 16949 Clause 8.5.1 — Control of Production; ISO 12647 — Colour Standards

description: Classification of the vehicle powertrain technology for this variant: ICE (Internal Combustion Engine), HEV (Hybrid Electric Vehicle), PHEV (Plug-in Hybrid Electric Vehicle), BEV (Battery Electric Vehicle), or FCEV (Fuel Cell Electric Vehicle). Drives assembly process routing, tooling requirements, and EPA/CARB emissions compliance classification.
reference: EPA 40 CFR Part 86 — Emissions Standards; CARB Title 13; SAE J1715

description: Current PPAP (Production Part Approval Process) approval status for this production variant. PPAP approval is a mandatory quality gate before SOP (Start of Production) under IATF 16949. Status values: NOT_REQUIRED, PENDING, SUBMITTED, APPROVED, REJECTED. Drives production release authorization in MES.
reference: AIAG PPAP 4th Edition; IATF 16949 Clause 8.3.4 — Design and Development Controls

description: Regulatory compliance region grouping for this production variant, used to apply the correct homologation ruleset: NAFTA (North America), EU (European Union), APAC (Asia-Pacific), LATAM (Latin America), MEA (Middle East & Africa), CHINA. Determines applicable NCAP (New Car Assessment Programme) standards, emissions regulations, and safety certification requirements.
reference: UNECE WP.29 — World Forum for Harmonization of Vehicle Regulations; NHTSA; Euro NCAP

description: Date on which this production variant is authorized to enter volume production (SOP — Start of Production). Used for production planning gate reviews, PPAP (Production Part Approval Process) milestone tracking, and supplier readiness confirmation.
reference: APQP (Advanced Product Quality Planning) Phase 4 — Product and Process Validation; IATF 16949

description: Standard labor hours required to complete the full assembly of this production variant from stamping through final assembly and PDI (Pre-Delivery Inspection). Used for production cost standard setting, workforce planning, and SAP CO (Controlling) cost object calculation.
reference: IATF 16949 Clause 8.5.1; SAP CO Standard Cost Estimation

description: Allocated takt time in seconds for building this production variant on the assigned production line. Represents the available production time divided by customer demand rate. Used for JIS (Just-in-Sequence) scheduling, capacity planning, and bottleneck identification in MES (Manufacturing Execution System).
reference: IATF 16949 Clause 8.5.1 — Control of Production; SAE J4000 — Lean Manufacturing

description: Cross-reference identifier for this production variant in Siemens Teamcenter PLM (Product Lifecycle Management) variant configuration module. Enables traceability between the manufacturing master data record and the engineering variant definition in the PLM system of record.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM — Variant Configuration; IATF 16949 Clause 7.5 — Documented Information

description: Type of transmission fitted in this production variant: AUTOMATIC (conventional automatic), MANUAL (manual gearbox), CVT (Continuously Variable Transmission), DCT (Dual-Clutch Transmission), or SINGLE_SPEED (for BEV/FCEV). Drives assembly routing to the correct powertrain marriage station and torque tooling specification.
reference: SAE J1297 — Automatic Transmission Functions; IATF 16949 Clause 8.5

description: Timestamp when this production variant record was most recently modified. Used for change data capture (CDC) in the Databricks Silver Layer pipeline and for engineering change audit trail compliance under IATF 16949 documented information control.
reference: IATF 16949 Clause 7.5 — Documented Information; ISO 9001 Clause 7.5

description: Indicates whether this production variant is equipped with V2X (Vehicle-to-Everything) communication hardware (True = V2X capable). Drives telematics module assembly sequencing, spectrum licensing compliance, and connected mobility service activation in Geotab/Bosch IoT platform.
reference: SAE J2945 — V2X Communications; ETSI ITS Standards; FCC 47 CFR Part 95

description: Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying this production variant within the manufacturing system. Used as the primary business key in MES (Manufacturing Execution System), PLM (Product Lifecycle Management), and SAP PP (Production Planning) for variant identification and cross-system referencing. Sourced from Siemens Teamcenter PLM variant configuration.
reference: IATF 16949 Clause 8.3 — Design and Development; SAP PP Variant Configuration

description: Human-readable descriptive name of the production variant (e.g., 'F-Series SuperCrew 4x4 BEV Long Range'). Used in build sequence planning, shop floor documentation, and JIS (Just-in-Sequence) scheduling displays.

Current lifecycle status of the production variant within the manufacturing master data: ACTIVE (currently buildable), PLANNED (approved for future SOP), INACTIVE (not currently scheduled), DISCONTINUED (past EOP), ON_HOLD (temporarily suspended pending engineering change or regulatory review). Drives MES build feasibility checks.
reference: IATF 16949 Clause 8.3 — Design and Development; SAP PP Material Status

description: OEM (Original Equipment Manufacturer) internal model code identifying the vehicle platform or nameplate (e.g., 'F150', 'RAV4', 'MODEL3'). Links the production variant to the vehicle program in PLM (Product Lifecycle Management) and SAP MM (Materials Management).
reference: SAE J1100 — Vehicle Dimensions; Siemens Teamcenter PLM

business_glossary_term: Worldwide Harmonised Light Vehicles Test Procedure (WLTP) Range (km)
description: Official WLTP (Worldwide Harmonised Light Vehicles Test Procedure) certified driving range in kilometres for BEV and PHEV variants. Null for ICE and HEV variants. Used for EU regulatory labelling, EPA range certification cross-reference, and consumer-facing range claims.
reference: WLTP Regulation (EU) 2017/1151; EPA 40 CFR Part 600 — Fuel Economy Labelling

description: Primary key for routing
Self-referencing FK on routing (alternate_routing_id)

Foreign key linking to quality.control_plan. Business justification: Manufacturing routings are paired with control plans in APQP/PPAP — the routing defines operation sequence while the control plan defines quality controls for those operations. IATF 16949 requires thi

Foreign key linking to engineering.part_master. Business justification: Routings are defined at the part/assembly level in automotive manufacturing — each routing governs how a specific part or subassembly is produced. Linking routing to part_master enables traceability f
Identifier of the manufacturing plant to which the routing belongs.

Foreign key linking to engineering.vehicle_program. Business justification: Manufacturing routings define operation sequences for a specific vehicle program. Program management, launch readiness reviews, and capacity planning all require knowing which vehicle_program a routin
User identifier who approved the routing change.
Date when the routing change was formally approved.
Business reason for the most recent change to the routing.
Applicable industry or internal compliance standard (e.g., ISO/TS 16949).
Timestamp when the routing record was first created in the master system.
Date when the routing is retired or superseded; null if open‑ended.
Date when the routing becomes valid for production planning.
True if this routing is the default for its product family.

Primary material category processed by the routing (e.g., steel, aluminum, composite).
Additional free‑form comments, special handling instructions, or exceptions.

High‑level product family (e.g., SUV, Truck, EV) that uses this routing.
True if the routing mandates a quality inspection step.

Human‑readable name of the routing used on shop floor displays and reports.

Current lifecycle state of the routing.
Category of the routing based on the manufacturing process it supports.
Safety classification of the routing based on hazard analysis.
Planned total processing time for the routing, expressed in minutes.
